{{Short description|Shrine in Hokkaido, Japan}} {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Sumiyoshi Shrine | image = [[File:Sumiyoshi Shrine of Otaru.jpg|250px|Sumiyoshi Shrine]]
[[Torii]] | location = [[Hokkaido]][[Otaru]] Suminoye 2-5-1 | coordinates = {{coord|43|10|58.6|N|141|0|11.6|E|region:JP-01_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{Plainlist| * [[Sokotutsuo no Mikoto|Sokotutsuo no Kami]] * [[Nakatutsuo no Mikoto|Nakatutsuo no Kami]] * [[Uwatsutsuo no Mikoto|Uwatsutsuo no Kami]] * [[Empress Jingū|Okitsuhime no Mikoto]] }} | type = {{Plainlist| * Former Prefectural Shrine * Beppyo Shrine }} | founded = [[1868]] (Meiji 1) | main hall= [[Sumiyoshi-zukuri]] | festival = July 15 | Rites = {{Plainlist| * Taishakagura Dedication festival * annual Grand festival * Autumn Grand festival }} | map = {{Switcher | {{maplink2|zoom=14|frame=yes|plain=yes|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|type=shape|stroke-width=2}} | location within Otaru | {{location map|Japan Hokkaido|lat_deg=43|lat_min=10|lat_sec=58.6|lon_deg=141|lon_min=0|lon_sec=11.6|label=Sumiyoshi Shrine|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|Alternativemap=Hokkaidō géolocalisation 000.svg}} | location within Hokkaido }} }} '''Sumiyoshi Shrine''' (住吉神社, ''Sumiyoshi Jinja'') is a [[Shinto]] shrine located in [[Otaru, Hokkaido]]. Its festival is on July 15.https://unga-plus.com/?pid=172674544 The [[kami]] Kamitsusu no O no Ōkami (上筒之男大神), Nakatsutsu no O no Ōkami (中筒之男大神), Sokotsutsu no O no Ōkami (底筒之男大神), Okinagaranushihime no Ōkami (息長足姫大神), Onamochi no Ōkami (大名持大神), [[Sukunabikona]] no Ōkami (少彦名大神) and others are enshrined here. ==Overview== {{Citation needed|date=March 2024}} '''Sumiyoshi Shrine''' (住吉神社, Sumiyoshi Jinja) is a Shinto shrine located in [[Otaru]], [[Hokkaido]], dedicated to the Sumiyoshi deities (Sokotutsuo no Kami, Nakatutsuo no Kami, Uwatsutsuo no Kami, and Okitsuhime no Mikoto). It is currently classified as a Beppyo Shrine under the [[Association of Shinto Shrines]], and its former rank under the modern shrine ranking system was Prefectural Shrine. == History == * [[1865]] (Keio 1): Shigeakira Kikuchi, a priest from Hakodate Hachiman Shrine, petitioned the Hakodate Bugyōsho to enshrine the Sumiyoshi deities as the general guardians for the Otarunai and Takashima areas. Permission was granted to enshrine them in Otarunai. * [[1868]] (Meiji 1): The sacred objects arrived in Otarunai. Due to the unprepared shrine grounds, they were temporarily enshrined at Itsukushima Shrine, where the consecration ceremony took place. * [[1875]] (Meiji 8): The shrine was classified as a Gōsha (village shrine). * [[1881]] (Meiji 14): The shrine buildings were constructed at the current location, and the shrine was relocated there. * [[1896]] (Meiji 29): The shrine was renamed from "Suminoe Shrine" to "Sumiyoshi Shrine". * [[1906]] (Meiji 39): The shrine was elevated to Prefectural Shrine status. * [[1955]] (Showa 30): The shrine was added to the list of Beppyo Shrines under the Association of Shinto Shrines. * [[1971]] (Showa 46): To commemorate the 100th anniversary of its establishment, the shrine buildings were renovated. * [[1997]] (Heisei 9): To mark the 130th anniversary, the shrine’s mikoshi (portable shrine), the largest in Hokkaido, was restored. * [[2007]] (Heisei 19): To celebrate the 140th anniversary, a mikoshi warehouse was built to house five mikoshi and one horse-drawn carriage. == annual Grand festival == The Sumiyoshi Shrine annual Grand festival, commonly known as the "Otaru festival," is considered one of the three major festivals in Otaru.{{Cite web | url = http://www.hokkaido-np.co.jp/news/chiiki/551341.html | title = 小樽・住吉神社例大祭 露店150店連なる 16日まで | work = [[Hokkaido Shimbun]] | publisher = Hokkaido Shimbunsha | date = 2014-07-15 | access-date = 2014-07-16 | archive-url = https://web.archive.org/web/20140726221521/https://www.hokkaido-np.co.jp/news/chiiki/551341.html | archive-date = 2014-07-26 }} == Access == * [[JR Hokkaido]] [[Hakodate Main Line]] [[Minami-Otaru Station]], an 8-minute walk * [[Hokkaido Chuo Bus]] (Otaru Depot, Shin Sapporo Depot, Satsuru Line), [[JR Hokkaido Bus]] (Satsuru Line), "Sumiyoshi Shrine Mae" bus stop, a 1-minute walk == Gallery == Sumiyoshi Shrine of Otaru - {{Short description|Shrine in Hokkaido, Japan}} {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Sumiyoshi Shrine | image = [[File:Sumiyoshi Shrine of Otaru.jpg|250px|Sumiyoshi Shrine]]
[[Torii]] | location = [[Hokkaido]][[Otaru]] Suminoye 2-5-1 | coordinates = {{coord|43|10|58.6|N|141|0|11.6|E|region:JP-01_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{Plainlist| * [[Sokotutsuo no Mikoto|Sokotutsuo no Kami]] * [[Nakatutsuo no Mikoto|Nakatutsuo no Kami]] * [[Uwatsutsuo no Mikoto|Uwatsutsuo no Kami]] * [[Empress Jingū|Okitsuhime no Mikoto]] }} | type = {{Plainlist| * Former Prefectural Shrine * Beppyo Shrine }} | founded = [[1868]] (Meiji 1) | main hall= [[Sumiyoshi-zukuri]] | festival = July 15 | Rites = {{Plainlist| * Taishakagura Dedication festival * annual Grand festival * Autumn Grand festival }} | map = {{Switcher | {{maplink2|zoom=14|frame=yes|plain=yes|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|type=shape|stroke-width=2}} | location within Otaru | {{location map|Japan Hokkaido|lat_deg=43|lat_min=10|lat_sec=58.6|lon_deg=141|lon_min=0|lon_sec=11.6|label=Sumiyoshi Shrine|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|Alternativemap=Hokkaidō géolocalisation 000.svg}} | location within Hokkaido }} }} '''Sumiyoshi Shrine''' (住吉神社, ''Sumiyoshi Jinja'') is a [[Shinto]] shrine located in [[Otaru, Hokkaido]]. Its festival is on July 15.https://unga-plus.com/?pid=172674544 The [[kami]] Kamitsusu no O no Ōkami (上筒之男大神), Nakatsutsu no O no Ōkami (中筒之男大神), Sokotsutsu no O no Ōkami (底筒之男大神), Okinagaranushihime no Ōkami (息長足姫大神), Onamochi no Ōkami (大名持大神), [[Sukunabikona]] no Ōkami (少彦名大神) and others are enshrined here. ==Overview== {{Citation needed|date=March 2024}} '''Sumiyoshi Shrine''' (住吉神社, Sumiyoshi Jinja) is a Shinto shrine located in [[Otaru]], [[Hokkaido]], dedicated to the Sumiyoshi deities (Sokotutsuo no Kami, Nakatutsuo no Kami, Uwatsutsuo no Kami, and Okitsuhime no Mikoto). It is currently classified as a Beppyo Shrine under the [[Association of Shinto Shrines]], and its former rank under the modern shrine ranking system was Prefectural Shrine. == History == * [[1865]] (Keio 1): Shigeakira Kikuchi, a priest from Hakodate Hachiman Shrine, petitioned the Hakodate Bugyōsho to enshrine the Sumiyoshi deities as the general guardians for the Otarunai and Takashima areas. Permission was granted to enshrine them in Otarunai. * [[1868]] (Meiji 1): The sacred objects arrived in Otarunai. Due to the unprepared shrine grounds, they were temporarily enshrined at Itsukushima Shrine, where the consecration ceremony took place. * [[1875]] (Meiji 8): The shrine was classified as a Gōsha (village shrine). * [[1881]] (Meiji 14): The shrine buildings were constructed at the current location, and the shrine was relocated there. * [[1896]] (Meiji 29): The shrine was renamed from "Suminoe Shrine" to "Sumiyoshi Shrine". * [[1906]] (Meiji 39): The shrine was elevated to Prefectural Shrine status. * [[1955]] (Showa 30): The shrine was added to the list of Beppyo Shrines under the Association of Shinto Shrines. * [[1971]] (Showa 46): To commemorate the 100th anniversary of its establishment, the shrine buildings were renovated. * [[1997]] (Heisei 9): To mark the 130th anniversary, the shrine’s mikoshi (portable shrine), the largest in Hokkaido, was restored. * [[2007]] (Heisei 19): To celebrate the 140th anniversary, a mikoshi warehouse was built to house five mikoshi and one horse-drawn carriage. == annual Grand festival == The Sumiyoshi Shrine annual Grand festival, commonly known as the "Otaru festival," is considered one of the three major festivals in Otaru.{{Cite web | url = http://www.hokkaido-np.co.jp/news/chiiki/551341.html | title = 小樽・住吉神社例大祭 露店150店連なる 16日まで | work = [[Hokkaido Shimbun]] | publisher = Hokkaido Shimbunsha | date = 2014-07-15 | access-date = 2014-07-16 | archive-url = https://web.archive.org/web/20140726221521/https://www.hokkaido-np.co.jp/news/chiiki/551341.html | archive-date = 2014-07-26 }} == Access == * [[JR Hokkaido]] [[Hakodate Main Line]] [[Minami-Otaru Station]], an 8-minute walk * [[Hokkaido Chuo Bus]] (Otaru Depot, Shin Sapporo Depot, Satsuru Line), [[JR Hokkaido Bus]] (Satsuru Line), "Sumiyoshi Shrine Mae" bus stop, a 1-minute walk == Gallery == Sumiyoshi Shrine of Otaru - Shamusho.jpg|Office (Otaru City Designated Historical Building) Sumiyoshi Shrine of Otaru - Haiden.jpg|Worship Hall Kataro Matsukawa 20210919.jpg|Statue of Katataro Matsukawa Monument to National Foundation Day at Sumiyoshi Shrine of Otaru.jpg|Monument to National Foundation Day Monument to Kitchen Knives at Sumiyoshi Shrine of Otaru.jpg|Kitchen Knife Monument Height Mesurement Stone at Sumiyoshi Shrine of Otaru.jpg|Height Measurement Stone and Child Blessing Dog File:第二次世界大戦前の住吉神社（北海道小樽市）.jpg|Sumiyoshi Shrine approach in the Taisho era (postcard dated July 9, 1919) == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat}} {{Osm box|w|665046858}} * {{Official website|url=http://www.otarusumiyoshijinja.or.jp/|name=Otaru General Guardian "Sumiyoshi Shrine"}} - Official website * [https://hokkaidojinjacho.jp/%e4%bd%8f%e5%90%89%e7%a5%9e%e7%a4%be-3/ Sumiyoshi Shrine] - Hokkaido Shrine Association {{-}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Sumiyoshi Shrine Otaru}} [[category:kk]][[category:Shinto shrines in Hokkaido]] [[category:kk]][[category:Sumiyoshi Shrines]] [[category:kk]][[category:History of Otaru]] [[category:kk]][[category:Buildings and structures in Otaru]] [[category:kk]][[category:Otaru City Designated Historical Buildings]] ==See also== *[[List of Shinto shrines in Japan#Hokkaid.C5.8D|List of Shinto shrines in Hokkaidō]] ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External links== *[https://hokkaidojinjacho.jp/住三吉神社/ Hokkaido Shinto listing] {{Shinto shrine}} {{coord missing|Hokkaidō Prefecture}} [[category:kk]][[category:Shinto shrines in Hokkaido]] {{Shinto-stub}} [[category:kk]][[category:Beppyo shrines]] [[category:kk]][[category:Ken-sha]] [[en:Sumiyoshi Shrine (Hokkaidō)]][[category:kk]][[category:Sumiyoshi shrines]] [[category:kk]] [[Category:Expanded pages from Wikipedia]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Sumiyoshi Shrine (Hokkaidō)]][[Category:dd]] [[en:Sumiyoshi Shrine (Hokkaidō)]]]] [[category:mm]] [[category:kk]][[category:kk]] [[category:kk]][[en:Sumiyoshi Shrine (Hokkaidō)]]]] [[category:mm]] [[category:kk]][[category:kk]] [[category:kk]][[en:Sumiyoshi Shrine (Hokkaidō)]]]] [[category:mm]] [[category:kk]][[category:kk]] [[Category:Kk]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Sumiyoshi Shrine (Hokkaidō)]][[Category:dd]] [[ja:住吉神社 (小樽市)]] [[en:Sumiyoshi Shrine (Hokkaidō)]][[en:Sumiyoshi Shrine (Hokkaidō)]][[en:Sumiyoshi Shrine (Hokkaidō)]] [[en:Sumiyoshi Shrine (Hokkaidō)]] {{Sumiyoshi Faith}}

{{Short description|Shrine in Hokkaido, Japan}} {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Sumiyoshi Shrine | image = [[File:Sumiyoshi Shrine of Otaru.jpg|250px|Sumiyoshi Shrine]]
[[Torii]] | location = [[Hokkaido]][[Otaru]] Suminoye 2-5-1 | coordinates = {{coord|43|10|58.6|N|141|0|11.6|E|region:JP-01_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{Plainlist| * [[Sokotutsuo no Mikoto|Sokotutsuo no Kami]] * [[Nakatutsuo no Mikoto|Nakatutsuo no Kami]] * [[Uwatsutsuo no Mikoto|Uwatsutsuo no Kami]] * [[Empress Jingū|Okitsuhime no Mikoto]] }} | type = {{Plainlist| * Former Prefectural Shrine * Beppyo Shrine }} | founded = [[1868]] (Meiji 1) | main hall= [[Sumiyoshi-zukuri]] | festival = July 15 | Rites = {{Plainlist| * Taishakagura Dedication festival * annual Grand festival * Autumn Grand festival }} | map = {{Switcher | {{maplink2|zoom=14|frame=yes|plain=yes|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|type=shape|stroke-width=2}} | location within Otaru | {{location map|Japan Hokkaido|lat_deg=43|lat_min=10|lat_sec=58.6|lon_deg=141|lon_min=0|lon_sec=11.6|label=Sumiyoshi Shrine|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|Alternativemap=Hokkaidō géolocalisation 000.svg}} | location within Hokkaido }} }} '''Sumiyoshi Shrine''' (住吉神社, ''Sumiyoshi Jinja'') is a [[Shinto]] shrine located in [[Otaru, Hokkaido]]. Its festival is on July 15.https://unga-plus.com/?pid=172674544 The [[kami]] Kamitsusu no O no Ōkami (上筒之男大神), Nakatsutsu no O no Ōkami (中筒之男大神), Sokotsutsu no O no Ōkami (底筒之男大神), Okinagaranushihime no Ōkami (息長足姫大神), Onamochi no Ōkami (大名持大神), [[Sukunabikona]] no Ōkami (少彦名大神) and others are enshrined here. ==Overview== {{Citation needed|date=March 2024}} '''Sumiyoshi Shrine''' (住吉神社, Sumiyoshi Jinja) is a Shinto shrine located in [[Otaru]], [[Hokkaido]], dedicated to the Sumiyoshi deities (Sokotutsuo no Kami, Nakatutsuo no Kami, Uwatsutsuo no Kami, and Okitsuhime no Mikoto). It is currently classified as a Beppyo Shrine under the [[Association of Shinto Shrines]], and its former rank under the modern shrine ranking system was Prefectural Shrine. == History == * [[1865]] (Keio 1): Shigeakira Kikuchi, a priest from Hakodate Hachiman Shrine, petitioned the Hakodate Bugyōsho to enshrine the Sumiyoshi deities as the general guardians for the Otarunai and Takashima areas. Permission was granted to enshrine them in Otarunai. * [[1868]] (Meiji 1): The sacred objects arrived in Otarunai. Due to the unprepared shrine grounds, they were temporarily enshrined at Itsukushima Shrine, where the consecration ceremony took place. * [[1875]] (Meiji 8): The shrine was classified as a Gōsha (village shrine). * [[1881]] (Meiji 14): The shrine buildings were constructed at the current location, and the shrine was relocated there. * [[1896]] (Meiji 29): The shrine was renamed from "Suminoe Shrine" to "Sumiyoshi Shrine". * [[1906]] (Meiji 39): The shrine was elevated to Prefectural Shrine status. * [[1955]] (Showa 30): The shrine was added to the list of Beppyo Shrines under the Association of Shinto Shrines. * [[1971]] (Showa 46): To commemorate the 100th anniversary of its establishment, the shrine buildings were renovated. * [[1997]] (Heisei 9): To mark the 130th anniversary, the shrine’s mikoshi (portable shrine), the largest in Hokkaido, was restored. * [[2007]] (Heisei 19): To celebrate the 140th anniversary, a mikoshi warehouse was built to house five mikoshi and one horse-drawn carriage. == annual Grand festival == The Sumiyoshi Shrine annual Grand festival, commonly known as the "Otaru festival," is considered one of the three major festivals in Otaru.{{Cite web | url = http://www.hokkaido-np.co.jp/news/chiiki/551341.html | title = 小樽・住吉神社例大祭 露店150店連なる 16日まで | work = [[Hokkaido Shimbun]] | publisher = Hokkaido Shimbunsha | date = 2014-07-15 | access-date = 2014-07-16 | archive-url = https://web.archive.org/web/20140726221521/https://www.hokkaido-np.co.jp/news/chiiki/551341.html | archive-date = 2014-07-26 }} == Access == * [[JR Hokkaido]] [[Hakodate Main Line]] [[Minami-Otaru Station]], an 8-minute walk * [[Hokkaido Chuo Bus]] (Otaru Depot, Shin Sapporo Depot, Satsuru Line), [[JR Hokkaido Bus]] (Satsuru Line), "Sumiyoshi Shrine Mae" bus stop, a 1-minute walk == Gallery == Sumiyoshi Shrine of Otaru - Shamusho.jpg|Office (Otaru City Designated Historical Building) Sumiyoshi Shrine of Otaru - Haiden.jpg|Worship Hall Kataro Matsukawa 20210919.jpg|Statue of Katataro Matsukawa Monument to National Foundation Day at Sumiyoshi Shrine of Otaru.jpg|Monument to National Foundation Day Monument to Kitchen Knives at Sumiyoshi Shrine of Otaru.jpg|Kitchen Knife Monument Height Mesurement Stone at Sumiyoshi Shrine of Otaru.jpg|Height Measurement Stone and Child Blessing Dog File:第二次世界大戦前の住吉神社（北海道小樽市）.jpg|Sumiyoshi Shrine approach in the Taisho era (postcard dated July 9, 1919) == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat}} {{Osm box|w|665046858}} * {{Official website|url=http://www.otarusumiyoshijinja.or.jp/|name=Otaru General Guardian "Sumiyoshi Shrine"}} - Official website * [https://hokkaidojinjacho.jp/%e4%bd%8f%e5%90%89%e7%a5%9e%e7%a4%be-3/ Sumiyoshi Shrine] - Hokkaido Shrine Association {{-}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Sumiyoshi Shrine Otaru}} [[category:kk]][[category:Shinto shrines in Hokkaido]] [[category:kk]][[category:Sumiyoshi Shrines]] [[category:kk]][[category:History of Otaru]] [[category:kk]][[category:Buildings and structures in Otaru]] [[category:kk]][[category:Otaru City Designated Historical Buildings]] ==See also== *[[List of Shinto shrines in Japan#Hokkaid.C5.8D|List of Shinto shrines in Hokkaidō]] ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External links== *[https://hokkaidojinjacho.jp/住三吉神社/ Hokkaido Shinto listing] {{Shinto shrine}} {{coord missing|Hokkaidō Prefecture}} [[category:kk]][[category:Shinto shrines in Hokkaido]] {{Shinto-stub}} [[category:kk]][[category:Beppyo shrines]] [[category:kk]][[category:Ken-sha]] [[en:Sumiyoshi Shrine (Hokkaidō)]][[category:kk]][[category:Sumiyoshi shrines]] [[category:kk]] [[Category:Expanded pages from wikipedia]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Sumiyoshi Shrine (Hokkaidō)]][[Category:dd]] [[en:Sumiyoshi Shrine (Hokkaidō)]]]] [[category:mm]] [[category:kk]][[category:kk]] [[category:kk]][[en:Sumiyoshi Shrine (Hokkaidō)]]]] [[category:mm]] [[category:kk]][[category:kk]] [[category:kk]][[en:Sumiyoshi Shrine (Hokkaidō)]]]] [[category:mm]] [[category:kk]][[category:kk]] [[Category:Kk]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Sumiyoshi Shrine (Hokkaidō)]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:bump-01-18]] [[Category:bump1-19]] [[ja:住吉神社 (小樽市)]] [[en:Sumiyoshi Shrine (Hokkaidō)]][[en:Sumiyoshi Shrine (Hokkaidō)]][[en:Sumiyoshi Shrine (Hokkaidō)]] [[en:Sumiyoshi Shrine (Hokkaidō)]] {{Sumiyoshi Faith}}

{{Short description|Shrine in Hokkaido, Japan}} {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Sumiyoshi Shrine | image = [[File:Sumiyoshi Shrine of Otaru.jpg|250px|Sumiyoshi Shrine]]
[[Torii]] | location = [[Hokkaido]][[Otaru]] Suminoye 2-5-1 | coordinates = {{coord|43|10|58.6|N|141|0|11.6|E|region:JP-01_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{Plainlist| * [[Sokotutsuo no Mikoto|Sokotutsuo no Kami]] * [[Nakatutsuo no Mikoto|Nakatutsuo no Kami]] * [[Uwatsutsuo no Mikoto|Uwatsutsuo no Kami]] * [[Empress Jingū|Okitsuhime no Mikoto]] }} | type = {{Plainlist| * Former Prefectural Shrine * Beppyo Shrine }} | founded = [[1868]] (Meiji 1) | main hall= [[Sumiyoshi-zukuri]] | festival = July 15 | Rites = {{Plainlist| * Taishakagura Dedication festival * annual Grand festival * Autumn Grand festival }} | map = {{Switcher | {{maplink2|zoom=14|frame=yes|plain=yes|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|type=shape|stroke-width=2}} | location within Otaru | {{location map|Japan Hokkaido|lat_deg=43|lat_min=10|lat_sec=58.6|lon_deg=141|lon_min=0|lon_sec=11.6|label=Sumiyoshi Shrine|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|Alternativemap=Hokkaidō géolocalisation 000.svg}} | location within Hokkaido }} }} '''Sumiyoshi Shrine''' (住吉神社, ''Sumiyoshi Jinja'') is a [[Shinto]] shrine located in [[Otaru, Hokkaido]]. Its festival is on July 15.https://unga-plus.com/?pid=172674544 The [[kami]] Kamitsusu no O no Ōkami (上筒之男大神), Nakatsutsu no O no Ōkami (中筒之男大神), Sokotsutsu no O no Ōkami (底筒之男大神), Okinagaranushihime no Ōkami (息長足姫大神), Onamochi no Ōkami (大名持大神), [[Sukunabikona]] no Ōkami (少彦名大神) and others are enshrined here. ==Overview== {{Citation needed|date=March 2024}} '''Sumiyoshi Shrine''' (住吉神社, Sumiyoshi Jinja) is a Shinto shrine located in [[Otaru]], [[Hokkaido]], dedicated to the Sumiyoshi deities (Sokotutsuo no Kami, Nakatutsuo no Kami, Uwatsutsuo no Kami, and Okitsuhime no Mikoto). It is currently classified as a Beppyo Shrine under the [[Association of Shinto Shrines]], and its former rank under the modern shrine ranking system was Prefectural Shrine. == History == * [[1865]] (Keio 1): Shigeakira Kikuchi, a priest from Hakodate Hachiman Shrine, petitioned the Hakodate Bugyōsho to enshrine the Sumiyoshi deities as the general guardians for the Otarunai and Takashima areas. Permission was granted to enshrine them in Otarunai. * [[1868]] (Meiji 1): The sacred objects arrived in Otarunai. Due to the unprepared shrine grounds, they were temporarily enshrined at Itsukushima Shrine, where the consecration ceremony took place. * [[1875]] (Meiji 8): The shrine was classified as a Gōsha (village shrine). * [[1881]] (Meiji 14): The shrine buildings were constructed at the current location, and the shrine was relocated there. * [[1896]] (Meiji 29): The shrine was renamed from "Suminoe Shrine" to "Sumiyoshi Shrine". * [[1906]] (Meiji 39): The shrine was elevated to Prefectural Shrine status. * [[1955]] (Showa 30): The shrine was added to the list of Beppyo Shrines under the Association of Shinto Shrines. * [[1971]] (Showa 46): To commemorate the 100th anniversary of its establishment, the shrine buildings were renovated. * [[1997]] (Heisei 9): To mark the 130th anniversary, the shrine’s mikoshi (portable shrine), the largest in Hokkaido, was restored. * [[2007]] (Heisei 19): To celebrate the 140th anniversary, a mikoshi warehouse was built to house five mikoshi and one horse-drawn carriage. == annual Grand festival == The Sumiyoshi Shrine annual Grand festival, commonly known as the "Otaru festival," is considered one of the three major festivals in Otaru.{{Cite web | url = http://www.hokkaido-np.co.jp/news/chiiki/551341.html | title = 小樽・住吉神社例大祭 露店150店連なる 16日まで | work = [[Hokkaido Shimbun]] | publisher = Hokkaido Shimbunsha | date = 2014-07-15 | access-date = 2014-07-16 | archive-url = https://web.archive.org/web/20140726221521/https://www.hokkaido-np.co.jp/news/chiiki/551341.html | archive-date = 2014-07-26 }} == Access == * [[JR Hokkaido]] [[Hakodate Main Line]] [[Minami-Otaru Station]], an 8-minute walk * [[Hokkaido Chuo Bus]] (Otaru Depot, Shin Sapporo Depot, Satsuru Line), [[JR Hokkaido Bus]] (Satsuru Line), "Sumiyoshi Shrine Mae" bus stop, a 1-minute walk == Gallery == Sumiyoshi Shrine of Otaru - Shamusho.jpg|Office (Otaru City Designated Historical Building) Sumiyoshi Shrine of Otaru - Haiden.jpg|Worship Hall Kataro Matsukawa 20210919.jpg|Statue of Katataro Matsukawa Monument to National Foundation Day at Sumiyoshi Shrine of Otaru.jpg|Monument to National Foundation Day Monument to Kitchen Knives at Sumiyoshi Shrine of Otaru.jpg|Kitchen Knife Monument Height Mesurement Stone at Sumiyoshi Shrine of Otaru.jpg|Height Measurement Stone and Child Blessing Dog File:第二次世界大戦前の住吉神社（北海道小樽市）.jpg|Sumiyoshi Shrine approach in the Taisho era (postcard dated July 9, 1919) == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat}} {{Osm box|w|665046858}} * {{Official website|url=http://www.otarusumiyoshijinja.or.jp/|name=Otaru General Guardian "Sumiyoshi Shrine"}} - Official website * [https://hokkaidojinjacho.jp/%e4%bd%8f%e5%90%89%e7%a5%9e%e7%a4%be-3/ Sumiyoshi Shrine] - Hokkaido Shrine Association {{-}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Sumiyoshi Shrine Otaru}} [[category:kk]][[category:Shinto shrines in Hokkaido]] [[category:kk]][[category:Sumiyoshi Shrines]] [[category:kk]][[category:History of Otaru]] [[category:kk]][[category:Buildings and structures in Otaru]] [[category:kk]][[category:Otaru City Designated Historical Buildings]] ==See also== *[[List of Shinto shrines in Japan#Hokkaid.C5.8D|List of Shinto shrines in Hokkaidō]] ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External links== *[https://hokkaidojinjacho.jp/住三吉神社/ Hokkaido Shinto listing] {{Shinto shrine}} {{coord missing|Hokkaidō Prefecture}} [[category:kk]][[category:Shinto shrines in Hokkaido]] {{Shinto-stub}} [[category:kk]][[category:Beppyo shrines]] [[category:kk]][[category:Ken-sha]] [[en:Sumiyoshi Shrine (Hokkaidō)]][[category:kk]][[category:Sumiyoshi shrines]] [[category:kk]] [[Category:Expanded pages from wikipedia]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Sumiyoshi Shrine (Hokkaidō)]][[Category:dd]] [[en:Sumiyoshi Shrine (Hokkaidō)]]]] [[category:mm]] [[category:kk]][[category:kk]] [[category:kk]][[en:Sumiyoshi Shrine (Hokkaidō)]]]] [[category:mm]] [[category:kk]][[category:kk]] [[category:kk]][[en:Sumiyoshi Shrine (Hokkaidō)]]]] [[category:mm]] [[category:kk]][[category:kk]] [[Category:Kk]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Sumiyoshi Shrine (Hokkaidō)]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:bump-01-18]] [[Category:bump1-19]] [[Category:refreshing categories imported on 2024-01-19]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:refreshing redirects 1-27]] [[ja:住吉神社 (小樽市)]] [[en:Sumiyoshi Shrine (Hokkaidō)]][[en:Sumiyoshi Shrine (Hokkaidō)]][[en:Sumiyoshi Shrine (Hokkaidō)]] [[en:Sumiyoshi Shrine (Hokkaidō)]] {{Sumiyoshi Faith}}

revision.text:
{{Short description|Shrine in Hokkaido, Japan}} {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Sumiyoshi Shrine | image = [[File:Sumiyoshi Shrine of Otaru.jpg|250px|Sumiyoshi Shrine]]
[[Torii]] | location = [[Hokkaido]][[Otaru]] Suminoye 2-5-1 | coordinates = {{coord|43|10|58.6|N|141|0|11.6|E|region:JP-01_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{Plainlist| * [[Sokotutsuo no Mikoto|Sokotutsuo no Kami]] * [[Nakatutsuo no Mikoto|Nakatutsuo no Kami]] * [[Uwatsutsuo no Mikoto|Uwatsutsuo no Kami]] * [[Empress Jingū|Okitsuhime no Mikoto]] }} | type = {{Plainlist| * Former Prefectural Shrine * Beppyo Shrine }} | founded = [[1868]] (Meiji 1) | main hall= [[Sumiyoshi-zukuri]] | festival = July 15 | Rites = {{Plainlist| * Taishakagura Dedication festival * annual Grand festival * Autumn Grand festival }} | map = {{Switcher | {{maplink2|zoom=14|frame=yes|plain=yes|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|type=shape|stroke-width=2}} | location within Otaru | {{location map|Japan Hokkaido|lat_deg=43|lat_min=10|lat_sec=58.6|lon_deg=141|lon_min=0|lon_sec=11.6|label=Sumiyoshi Shrine|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|Alternativemap=Hokkaidō géolocalisation 000.svg}} | location within Hokkaido }} }} '''Sumiyoshi Shrine''' (住吉神社, ''Sumiyoshi Jinja'') is a [[Shinto]] shrine located in [[Otaru, Hokkaido]]. Its festival is on July 15.https://unga-plus.com/?pid=172674544 The [[kami]] Kamitsusu no O no Ōkami (上筒之男大神), Nakatsutsu no O no Ōkami (中筒之男大神), Sokotsutsu no O no Ōkami (底筒之男大神), Okinagaranushihime no Ōkami (息長足姫大神), Onamochi no Ōkami (大名持大神), [[Sukunabikona]] no Ōkami (少彦名大神) and others are enshrined here. ==Overview== {{Citation needed|date=March 2024}} '''Sumiyoshi Shrine''' (住吉神社, Sumiyoshi Jinja) is a Shinto shrine located in [[Otaru]], [[Hokkaido]], dedicated to the Sumiyoshi deities (Sokotutsuo no Kami, Nakatutsuo no Kami, Uwatsutsuo no Kami, and Okitsuhime no Mikoto). It is currently classified as a Beppyo Shrine under the [[Association of Shinto Shrines]], and its former rank under the modern shrine ranking system was Prefectural Shrine. == History == * [[1865]] (Keio 1): Shigeakira Kikuchi, a priest from Hakodate Hachiman Shrine, petitioned the Hakodate Bugyōsho to enshrine the Sumiyoshi deities as the general guardians for the Otarunai and Takashima areas. Permission was granted to enshrine them in Otarunai. * [[1868]] (Meiji 1): The sacred objects arrived in Otarunai. Due to the unprepared shrine grounds, they were temporarily enshrined at Itsukushima Shrine, where the consecration ceremony took place. * [[1875]] (Meiji 8): The shrine was classified as a Gōsha (village shrine). * [[1881]] (Meiji 14): The shrine buildings were constructed at the current location, and the shrine was relocated there. * [[1896]] (Meiji 29): The shrine was renamed from "Suminoe Shrine" to "Sumiyoshi Shrine". * [[1906]] (Meiji 39): The shrine was elevated to Prefectural Shrine status. * [[1955]] (Showa 30): The shrine was added to the list of Beppyo Shrines under the Association of Shinto Shrines. * [[1971]] (Showa 46): To commemorate the 100th anniversary of its establishment, the shrine buildings were renovated. * [[1997]] (Heisei 9): To mark the 130th anniversary, the shrine’s mikoshi (portable shrine), the largest in Hokkaido, was restored. * [[2007]] (Heisei 19): To celebrate the 140th anniversary, a mikoshi warehouse was built to house five mikoshi and one horse-drawn carriage. == annual Grand festival == The Sumiyoshi Shrine annual Grand festival, commonly known as the "Otaru festival," is considered one of the three major festivals in Otaru.{{Cite web | url = http://www.hokkaido-np.co.jp/news/chiiki/551341.html | title = 小樽・住吉神社例大祭 露店150店連なる 16日まで | work = [[Hokkaido Shimbun]] | publisher = Hokkaido Shimbunsha | date = 2014-07-15 | access-date = 2014-07-16 | archive-url = https://web.archive.org/web/20140726221521/https://www.hokkaido-np.co.jp/news/chiiki/551341.html | archive-date = 2014-07-26 }} == Access == * [[JR Hokkaido]] [[Hakodate Main Line]] [[Minami-Otaru Station]], an 8-minute walk * [[Hokkaido Chuo Bus]] (Otaru Depot, Shin Sapporo Depot, Satsuru Line), [[JR Hokkaido Bus]] (Satsuru Line), "Sumiyoshi Shrine Mae" bus stop, a 1-minute walk == Gallery == Sumiyoshi Shrine of Otaru - Shamusho.jpg|Office (Otaru City Designated Historical Building) Sumiyoshi Shrine of Otaru - Haiden.jpg|Worship Hall Kataro Matsukawa 20210919.jpg|Statue of Katataro Matsukawa Monument to National Foundation Day at Sumiyoshi Shrine of Otaru.jpg|Monument to National Foundation Day Monument to Kitchen Knives at Sumiyoshi Shrine of Otaru.jpg|Kitchen Knife Monument Height Mesurement Stone at Sumiyoshi Shrine of Otaru.jpg|Height Measurement Stone and Child Blessing Dog File:第二次世界大戦前の住吉神社（北海道小樽市）.jpg|Sumiyoshi Shrine approach in the Taisho era (postcard dated July 9, 1919) == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat}} {{Osm box|w|665046858}} * {{Official website|url=http://www.otarusumiyoshijinja.or.jp/|name=Otaru General Guardian "Sumiyoshi Shrine"}} - Official website * [https://hokkaidojinjacho.jp/%e4%bd%8f%e5%90%89%e7%a5%9e%e7%a4%be-3/ Sumiyoshi Shrine] - Hokkaido Shrine Association {{-}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Sumiyoshi Shrine Otaru}} [[category:kk]][[category:Shinto shrines in Hokkaido]] [[category:kk]][[category:Sumiyoshi Shrines]] of Shinto shrines in Japan#Hokkaid.C5.8D|List of Shinto shrines in Hokkaidō]] ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External links== *[https://hokkaidojinjacho.jp/住三吉神社/ Hokkaido Shinto listing] {{Shinto Shrine (Hokkaidō)]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Sumiyoshi Shrine (Hokkaidō)]][[Category:dd]] [[en:Sumiyoshi Shrine (Hokkaidō)]]]] [[category:mm]] [[category:kk]][[category:kk]] [[category:kk]][[en:Sumiyoshi Shrine (Hokkaidō)]]]] [[category:mm]] [[category:kk]][[category:kk]] [[category:kk]][[en:Sumiyoshi Shrine (Hokkaidō)]]]] [[category:mm]] (Hokkaidō)]][[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] [[ja:住吉神社 (小樽市)]] [[en:Sumiyoshi Shrine (Hokkaidō)]][[en:Sumiyoshi Shrine (Hokkaidō)]][[en:Sumiyoshi Shrine (Hokkaidō)]] {{Short description|Shrine in Hokkaido, Japan}} {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Sumiyoshi Shrine | image = [[File:Sumiyoshi Shrine of Otaru.jpg|250px|Sumiyoshi Shrine]]
[[Torii]] | location = [[Hokkaido]][[Otaru]] Suminoye 2-5-1 | coordinates = {{coord|43|10|58.6|N|141|0|11.6|E|region:JP-01_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{Plainlist| * [[Sokotutsuo no Mikoto|Sokotutsuo no Kami]] * [[Nakatutsuo no Mikoto|Nakatutsuo no Kami]] * [[Uwatsutsuo no Mikoto|Uwatsutsuo no Kami]] * [[Empress Jingū|Okitsuhime no Mikoto]] }} | type = {{Plainlist| * Former Prefectural Shrine * Beppyo Shrine }} | founded = [[1868]] (Meiji 1) | main hall= [[Sumiyoshi-zukuri]] | festival = July 15 | Rites = {{Plainlist| * Taishakagura Dedication festival * annual Grand festival * Autumn Grand festival }} | map = {{Switcher | {{maplink2|zoom=14|frame=yes|plain=yes|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|type=shape|stroke-width=2}} | location within Otaru | {{location map|Japan Hokkaido|lat_deg=43|lat_min=10|lat_sec=58.6|lon_deg=141|lon_min=0|lon_sec=11.6|label=Sumiyoshi Shrine|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|Alternativemap=Hokkaidō géolocalisation 000.svg}} | location within Hokkaido }} }} '''Sumiyoshi Shrine''' (住吉神社, ''Sumiyoshi Jinja'') is a [[Shinto]] shrine located in [[Otaru, Hokkaido]]. Its festival is on July 15.https://unga-plus.com/?pid=172674544 The [[kami]] Kamitsusu no O no Ōkami (上筒之男大神), Nakatsutsu no O no Ōkami (中筒之男大神), Sokotsutsu no O no Ōkami (底筒之男大神), Okinagaranushihime no Ōkami (息長足姫大神), Onamochi no Ōkami (大名持大神), [[Sukunabikona]] no Ōkami (少彦名大神) and others are enshrined here. ==Overview== {{Citation needed|date=March 2024}} '''Sumiyoshi Shrine''' (住吉神社, Sumiyoshi Jinja) is a Shinto shrine located in [[Otaru]], [[Hokkaido]], dedicated to the Sumiyoshi deities (Sokotutsuo no Kami, Nakatutsuo no Kami, Uwatsutsuo no Kami, and Okitsuhime no Mikoto). It is currently classified as a Beppyo Shrine under the [[Association of Shinto Shrines]], and its former rank under the modern shrine ranking system was Prefectural Shrine. == History == * [[1865]] (Keio 1): Shigeakira Kikuchi, a priest from Hakodate Hachiman Shrine, petitioned the Hakodate Bugyōsho to enshrine the Sumiyoshi deities as the general guardians for the Otarunai and Takashima areas. Permission was granted to enshrine them in Otarunai. * [[1868]] (Meiji 1): The sacred objects arrived in Otarunai. Due to the unprepared shrine grounds, they were temporarily enshrined at Itsukushima Shrine, where the consecration ceremony took place. * [[1875]] (Meiji 8): The shrine was classified as a Gōsha (village shrine). * [[1881]] (Meiji 14): The shrine buildings were constructed at the current location, and the shrine was relocated there. * [[1896]] (Meiji 29): The shrine was renamed from "Suminoe Shrine" to "Sumiyoshi Shrine". * [[1906]] (Meiji 39): The shrine was elevated to Prefectural Shrine status. * [[1955]] (Showa 30): The shrine was added to the list of Beppyo Shrines under the Association of Shinto Shrines. * [[1971]] (Showa 46): To commemorate the 100th anniversary of its establishment, the shrine buildings were renovated. * [[1997]] (Heisei 9): To mark the 130th anniversary, the shrine’s mikoshi (portable shrine), the largest in Hokkaido, was restored. * [[2007]] (Heisei 19): To celebrate the 140th anniversary, a mikoshi warehouse was built to house five mikoshi and one horse-drawn carriage. == annual Grand festival == The Sumiyoshi Shrine annual Grand festival, commonly known as the "Otaru festival," is considered one of the three major festivals in Otaru.{{Cite web | url = http://www.hokkaido-np.co.jp/news/chiiki/551341.html | title = 小樽・住吉神社例大祭 露店150店連なる 16日まで | work = [[Hokkaido Shimbun]] | publisher = Hokkaido Shimbunsha | date = 2014-07-15 | access-date = 2014-07-16 | archive-url = https://web.archive.org/web/20140726221521/https://www.hokkaido-np.co.jp/news/chiiki/551341.html | archive-date = 2014-07-26 }} == Access == * [[JR Hokkaido]] [[Hakodate Main Line]] [[Minami-Otaru Station]], an 8-minute walk * [[Hokkaido Chuo Bus]] (Otaru Depot, Shin Sapporo Depot, Satsuru Line), [[JR Hokkaido Bus]] (Satsuru Line), "Sumiyoshi Shrine Mae" bus stop, a 1-minute walk == Gallery == Sumiyoshi Shrine of Otaru - Shamusho.jpg|Office (Otaru City Designated Historical Building) Sumiyoshi Shrine of Otaru - Haiden.jpg|Worship Hall Kataro Matsukawa {{Short description|Shrine in Hokkaido, Japan}} {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Sumiyoshi Shrine | image = [[File:Sumiyoshi Shrine of Otaru.jpg|250px|Sumiyoshi Shrine]]
[[Torii]] | location = [[Hokkaido]][[Otaru]] Suminoye 2-5-1 | coordinates = {{coord|43|10|58.6|N|141|0|11.6|E|region:JP-01_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{Plainlist| * [[Sokotutsuo no Mikoto|Sokotutsuo no Kami]] * [[Nakatutsuo no Mikoto|Nakatutsuo no Kami]] * [[Uwatsutsuo no Mikoto|Uwatsutsuo no Kami]] * [[Empress Jingū|Okitsuhime no Mikoto]] }} | type = {{Plainlist| * Former Prefectural Shrine * Beppyo Shrine }} | founded = [[1868]] (Meiji 1) | main hall= [[Sumiyoshi-zukuri]] | festival = July 15 | Rites = {{Plainlist| * Taishakagura Dedication festival * annual Grand festival * Autumn Grand festival }} | map = {{Switcher | {{maplink2|zoom=14|frame=yes|plain=yes|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|type=shape|stroke-width=2}} | location within Otaru | {{location map|Japan Hokkaido|lat_deg=43|lat_min=10|lat_sec=58.6|lon_deg=141|lon_min=0|lon_sec=11.6|label=Sumiyoshi Shrine|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|Alternativemap=Hokkaidō géolocalisation 000.svg}} | location within Hokkaido }} }} '''Sumiyoshi Shrine''' (住吉神社, ''Sumiyoshi Jinja'') is a [[Shinto]] shrine located in [[Otaru, Hokkaido]]. Its festival is on July 15.https://unga-plus.com/?pid=172674544 The [[kami]] Kamitsusu no O no Ōkami (上筒之男大神), Nakatsutsu no O no Ōkami (中筒之男大神), Sokotsutsu no O no Ōkami (底筒之男大神), Okinagaranushihime no Ōkami (息長足姫大神), Onamochi no Ōkami (大名持大神), [[Sukunabikona]] no Ōkami (少彦名大神) and others are enshrined here. ==Overview== {{Citation needed|date=March 2024}} '''Sumiyoshi Shrine''' (住吉神社, Sumiyoshi Jinja) is a Shinto shrine located in [[Otaru]], [[Hokkaido]], dedicated to the Sumiyoshi deities (Sokotutsuo no Kami, Nakatutsuo no Kami, Uwatsutsuo no Kami, and Okitsuhime no Mikoto). It is currently classified as a Beppyo Shrine under the [[Association of Shinto Shrines]], and its former rank under the modern shrine ranking system was Prefectural Shrine. == History == * [[1865]] (Keio 1): Shigeakira Kikuchi, a priest from Hakodate Hachiman Shrine, petitioned the Hakodate Bugyōsho to enshrine the Sumiyoshi deities as the general guardians for the Otarunai and Takashima areas. Permission was granted to enshrine them in Otarunai. * [[1868]] (Meiji 1): The sacred objects arrived in Otarunai. Due to the unprepared shrine grounds, they were temporarily enshrined at Itsukushima Shrine, where the consecration ceremony took place. * [[1875]] (Meiji 8): The shrine was classified as a Gōsha (village shrine). * [[1881]] (Meiji 14): The shrine buildings were constructed at the current location, and the shrine was relocated there. * [[1896]] (Meiji 29): The shrine was renamed from "Suminoe Shrine" to "Sumiyoshi Shrine". * [[1906]] (Meiji 39): The shrine was elevated to Prefectural Shrine status. * [[1955]] (Showa 30): The shrine was added to the list of Beppyo Shrines under the Association of Shinto Shrines. * [[1971]] (Showa 46): To commemorate the 100th anniversary of its establishment, the shrine buildings were renovated. * [[1997]] (Heisei 9): To mark the 130th anniversary, the shrine’s mikoshi (portable shrine), the largest in Hokkaido, was restored. * [[2007]] (Heisei 19): To celebrate the 140th anniversary, a mikoshi warehouse was built to house five mikoshi and one horse-drawn carriage. == annual Grand festival == The Sumiyoshi Shrine annual Grand festival, commonly known as the "Otaru festival," is considered one of the three major festivals in Otaru.{{Cite web | url = http://www.hokkaido-np.co.jp/news/chiiki/551341.html | title = 小樽・住吉神社例大祭 露店150店連なる 16日まで | work = [[Hokkaido Shimbun]] | publisher = Hokkaido Shimbunsha | date = 2014-07-15 | access-date = 2014-07-16 | archive-url = https://web.archive.org/web/20140726221521/https://www.hokkaido-np.co.jp/news/chiiki/551341.html | archive-date = 2014-07-26 }} == Access == * [[JR Hokkaido]] [[Hakodate Main Line]] [[Minami-Otaru Station]], an 8-minute walk * [[Hokkaido Chuo Bus]] (Otaru Depot, Shin Sapporo Depot, Satsuru Line), [[JR Hokkaido Bus]] (Satsuru Line), "Sumiyoshi Shrine Mae" bus stop, a 1-minute walk == Gallery == Sumiyoshi Shrine of Otaru - Shamusho.jpg|Office (Otaru City Designated Historical Building) Sumiyoshi Shrine of Otaru - Haiden.jpg|Worship Hall Kataro Matsukawa 20210919.jpg|Statue of Katataro Matsukawa Monument to National Foundation Day at Sumiyoshi Shrine of Otaru.jpg|Monument to National Foundation Day Monument to Kitchen Knives at Sumiyoshi Shrine of Otaru.jpg|Kitchen Knife Monument Height Mesurement Stone at Sumiyoshi Shrine of Otaru.jpg|Height Measurement Stone and Child Blessing Dog File:第二次世界大戦前の住吉神社（北海道小樽市）.jpg|Sumiyoshi Shrine approach in the Taisho era (postcard dated July 9, 1919) == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat}} {{Osm box|w|665046858}} * {{Official Shrine] - Hokkaido Shrine Association {{-}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Sumiyoshi Shrine Otaru}} [[category:kk]][[category:Shinto shrines in Hokkaido]] [[category:kk]][[category:Sumiyoshi Shrines]] revision.text:
{{Short description|Shrine in Hokkaido, Japan}} {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Sumiyoshi Shrine | image = [[File:Sumiyoshi Shrine of Otaru.jpg|250px|Sumiyoshi Shrine]]
[[Torii]] | location = [[Hokkaido]][[Otaru]] Suminoye 2-5-1 | coordinates = {{coord|43|10|58.6|N|141|0|11.6|E|region:JP-01_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{Plainlist| * [[Sokotutsuo no Mikoto|Sokotutsuo no Kami]] * [[Nakatutsuo no Mikoto|Nakatutsuo no Kami]] * [[Uwatsutsuo no Mikoto|Uwatsutsuo no Kami]] * [[Empress Jingū|Okitsuhime no Mikoto]] }} | type = {{Plainlist| * Former Prefectural Shrine * Beppyo Shrine }} | founded = [[1868]] (Meiji 1) | main hall= [[Sumiyoshi-zukuri]] | festival = July 15 | Rites = {{Plainlist| * Taishakagura Dedication festival * annual Grand festival * Autumn Grand festival }} | map = {{Switcher | {{maplink2|zoom=14|frame=yes|plain=yes|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|type=shape|stroke-width=2}} | location within Otaru | {{location map|Japan Hokkaido|lat_deg=43|lat_min=10|lat_sec=58.6|lon_deg=141|lon_min=0|lon_sec=11.6|label=Sumiyoshi Shrine|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|Alternativemap=Hokkaidō géolocalisation 000.svg}} | location within Hokkaido }} }} '''Sumiyoshi Shrine''' (住吉神社, ''Sumiyoshi Jinja'') is a [[Shinto]] shrine located in [[Otaru, Hokkaido]]. Its festival is on July 15.https://unga-plus.com/?pid=172674544 The [[kami]] Kamitsusu no O no Ōkami (上筒之男大神), Nakatsutsu no O no Ōkami (中筒之男大神), Sokotsutsu no O no Ōkami (底筒之男大神), Okinagaranushihime no Ōkami (息長足姫大神), Onamochi no Ōkami (大名持大神), [[Sukunabikona]] no Ōkami (少彦名大神) and others are enshrined here. ==Overview== {{Citation needed|date=March 2024}} '''Sumiyoshi Shrine''' (住吉神社, Sumiyoshi Jinja) is a Shinto shrine located in [[Otaru]], [[Hokkaido]], dedicated to the Sumiyoshi deities (Sokotutsuo no Kami, Nakatutsuo no Kami, Uwatsutsuo no Kami, and Okitsuhime no Mikoto). It is currently classified as a Beppyo Shrine under the [[Association of Shinto Shrines]], and its former rank under the modern shrine ranking system was Prefectural Shrine. == History == * [[1865]] (Keio 1): Shigeakira Kikuchi, a priest from Hakodate Hachiman Shrine, petitioned the Hakodate Bugyōsho to enshrine the Sumiyoshi deities as the general guardians for the Otarunai and Takashima areas. Permission was granted to enshrine them in Otarunai. * [[1868]] (Meiji 1): The sacred objects arrived in Otarunai. Due to the unprepared shrine grounds, they were temporarily enshrined at Itsukushima Shrine, where the consecration ceremony took place. * [[1875]] (Meiji 8): The shrine was classified as a Gōsha (village shrine). * [[1881]] (Meiji 14): The shrine buildings were constructed at the current location, and the shrine was relocated there. * [[1896]] (Meiji 29): The shrine was renamed from "Suminoe Shrine" to "Sumiyoshi Shrine". * [[1906]] (Meiji 39): The shrine was elevated to Prefectural Shrine status. * [[1955]] (Showa 30): The shrine was added to the list of Beppyo Shrines under the Association of Shinto Shrines. * [[1971]] (Showa 46): To commemorate the 100th anniversary of its establishment, the shrine buildings were renovated. * [[1997]] (Heisei 9): To mark the 130th anniversary, the shrine’s mikoshi (portable shrine), the largest in Hokkaido, was restored. * [[2007]] (Heisei 19): To celebrate the 140th anniversary, a mikoshi warehouse was built to house five mikoshi and one horse-drawn carriage. == annual Grand festival == The Sumiyoshi Shrine annual Grand festival, commonly known as the "Otaru festival," is considered one of the three major festivals in Otaru.{{Cite web | url = http://www.hokkaido-np.co.jp/news/chiiki/551341.html | title = 小樽・住吉神社例大祭 露店150店連なる 16日まで | work = [[Hokkaido Shimbun]] | publisher = Hokkaido Shimbunsha | date = 2014-07-15 | access-date = 2014-07-16 | archive-url = https://web.archive.org/web/20140726221521/https://www.hokkaido-np.co.jp/news/chiiki/551341.html | archive-date = 2014-07-26 }} == Access == * [[JR Hokkaido]] [[Hakodate Main Line]] [[Minami-Otaru Station]], an 8-minute walk * [[Hokkaido Chuo Bus]] (Otaru Depot, Shin Sapporo Depot, Satsuru Line), [[JR Hokkaido Bus]] (Satsuru Line), "Sumiyoshi Shrine Mae" bus stop, a 1-minute walk == Gallery == Sumiyoshi Shrine of Otaru - Shamusho.jpg|Office (Otaru City Designated Historical Building) Sumiyoshi Shrine of Otaru - Haiden.jpg|Worship Hall Kataro Matsukawa 20210919.jpg|Statue of Katataro Matsukawa Monument to National Foundation Day at Sumiyoshi Shrine of Otaru.jpg|Monument to National Foundation Day Monument to Kitchen Knives at Sumiyoshi Shrine of Otaru.jpg|Kitchen Knife Monument Height Mesurement Stone at Sumiyoshi Shrine of Otaru.jpg|Height Measurement Stone and Child Blessing Dog File:第二次世界大戦前の住吉神社（北海道小樽市）.jpg|Sumiyoshi Shrine approach in the Taisho era (postcard dated July 9, 1919) == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat}} {{Osm box|w|665046858}} * {{Official website|url=http://www.otarusumiyoshijinja.or.jp/|name=Otaru General Guardian "Sumiyoshi Shrine"}} - Official website * [https://hokkaidojinjacho.jp/%e4%bd%8f%e5%90%89%e7%a5%9e%e7%a4%be-3/ Sumiyoshi Shrine] - Hokkaido Shrine Association {{-}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Sumiyoshi Shrine Otaru}} [[category:kk]][[category:Shinto shrines in Hokkaido]] [[category:kk]][[category:Sumiyoshi Shrines]] {{Short description|Shrine in Hokkaido, Japan}} {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Sumiyoshi Shrine | image = [[File:Sumiyoshi Shrine of Otaru.jpg|250px|Sumiyoshi Shrine]]
[[Torii]] | location = [[Hokkaido]][[Otaru]] Suminoye 2-5-1 | coordinates = {{coord|43|10|58.6|N|141|0|11.6|E|region:JP-01_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{Plainlist| * [[Sokotutsuo no Mikoto|Sokotutsuo no Kami]] * [[Nakatutsuo no Mikoto|Nakatutsuo no Kami]] * [[Uwatsutsuo no Mikoto|Uwatsutsuo no Kami]] * [[Empress Jingū|Okitsuhime no Mikoto]] }} | type = {{Plainlist| * Former Prefectural Shrine * Beppyo Shrine }} | founded = [[1868]] (Meiji 1) | main hall= [[Sumiyoshi-zukuri]] | festival = July 15 | Rites = {{Plainlist| * Taishakagura Dedication festival * annual Grand festival * Autumn Grand festival }} | map = {{Switcher | {{maplink2|zoom=14|frame=yes|plain=yes|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|type=shape|stroke-width=2}} | location within Otaru | {{location map|Japan Hokkaido|lat_deg=43|lat_min=10|lat_sec=58.6|lon_deg=141|lon_min=0|lon_sec=11.6|label=Sumiyoshi Shrine|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|Alternativemap=Hokkaidō géolocalisation 000.svg}} | location within Hokkaido }} }} '''Sumiyoshi Shrine''' (住吉神社, ''Sumiyoshi Jinja'') is a [[Shinto]] shrine located in [[Otaru, Hokkaido]]. Its festival is on July 15.https://unga-plus.com/?pid=172674544 The [[kami]] Kamitsusu no O no Ōkami (上筒之男大神), Nakatsutsu no O no Ōkami (中筒之男大神), Sokotsutsu no O no Ōkami (底筒之男大神), Okinagaranushihime no Ōkami (息長足姫大神), Onamochi no Ōkami (大名持大神), [[Sukunabikona]] no Ōkami (少彦名大神) and others are enshrined here. ==Overview== {{Citation needed|date=March 2024}} '''Sumiyoshi Shrine''' (住吉神社, Sumiyoshi Jinja) is a Shinto shrine located in [[Otaru]], [[Hokkaido]], dedicated to the Sumiyoshi deities (Sokotutsuo no Kami, Nakatutsuo no Kami, Uwatsutsuo no Kami, and Okitsuhime no Mikoto). It is currently classified as a Beppyo Shrine under the [[Association of Shinto Shrines]], and its former rank under the modern shrine ranking system was Prefectural Shrine. == History == * [[1865]] (Keio 1): Shigeakira Kikuchi, a priest from Hakodate Hachiman Shrine, petitioned the Hakodate Bugyōsho to enshrine the Sumiyoshi deities as the general guardians for the Otarunai and Takashima areas. Permission was granted to enshrine them in Otarunai. * [[1868]] (Meiji 1): The sacred objects arrived in Otarunai. Due to the unprepared shrine grounds, they were temporarily enshrined at Itsukushima Shrine, where the consecration ceremony took place. * [[1875]] (Meiji 8): The shrine was classified as a Gōsha (village shrine). * [[1881]] (Meiji 14): The shrine buildings were constructed at the current location, and the shrine was relocated there. * [[1896]] (Meiji 29): The shrine was renamed from "Suminoe Shrine" to "Sumiyoshi Shrine". * [[1906]] (Meiji 39): The shrine was elevated to Prefectural Shrine status. * [[1955]] (Showa 30): The shrine was added to the list of Beppyo Shrines under the Association of Shinto Shrines. * [[1971]] (Showa 46): To commemorate the 100th anniversary of its establishment, the shrine buildings were renovated. * [[1997]] (Heisei 9): To mark the 130th anniversary, the shrine’s mikoshi (portable shrine), the largest in Hokkaido, was restored. * [[2007]] (Heisei 19): To celebrate the 140th anniversary, a mikoshi warehouse was built to house five mikoshi and one horse-drawn carriage. == annual Grand festival == The Sumiyoshi Shrine annual Grand festival, commonly known as the "Otaru festival," is considered one of the three major festivals in Otaru.{{Cite web | url = http://www.hokkaido-np.co.jp/news/chiiki/551341.html | title = 小樽・住吉神社例大祭 露店150店連なる 16日まで | work = [[Hokkaido Shimbun]] | publisher = Hokkaido Shimbunsha | date = 2014-07-15 | access-date = 2014-07-16 | archive-url = https://web.archive.org/web/20140726221521/https://www.hokkaido-np.co.jp/news/chiiki/551341.html | archive-date = 2014-07-26 }} == Access == * [[JR Hokkaido]] [[Hakodate Main Line]] [[Minami-Otaru Station]], an 8-minute walk * [[Hokkaido Chuo Bus]] (Otaru Depot, Shin Sapporo Depot, Satsuru Line), [[JR Hokkaido Bus]] (Satsuru Line), "Sumiyoshi Shrine Mae" bus stop, a 1-minute walk == Gallery == Sumiyoshi Shrine of Otaru - Shamusho.jpg|Office (Otaru City Designated Historical Building) Sumiyoshi Shrine of Otaru - Haiden.jpg|Worship Hall Kataro Matsukawa {{Short description|Shrine in Hokkaido, Japan}} {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Sumiyoshi Shrine | image = [[File:Sumiyoshi Shrine of Otaru.jpg|250px|Sumiyoshi Shrine]]
[[Torii]] | location = [[Hokkaido]][[Otaru]] Suminoye 2-5-1 | coordinates = {{coord|43|10|58.6|N|141|0|11.6|E|region:JP-01_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{Plainlist| * [[Sokotutsuo no Mikoto|Sokotutsuo no Kami]] * [[Nakatutsuo no Mikoto|Nakatutsuo no Kami]] * [[Uwatsutsuo no Mikoto|Uwatsutsuo no Kami]] * [[Empress Jingū|Okitsuhime no Mikoto]] }} | type = {{Plainlist| * Former Prefectural Shrine * Beppyo Shrine }} | founded = [[1868]] (Meiji 1) | main hall= [[Sumiyoshi-zukuri]] | festival = July 15 | Rites = {{Plainlist| * Taishakagura Dedication festival * annual Grand festival * Autumn Grand festival }} | map = {{Switcher | {{maplink2|zoom=14|frame=yes|plain=yes|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|type=shape|stroke-width=2}} | location within Otaru | {{location map|Japan Hokkaido|lat_deg=43|lat_min=10|lat_sec=58.6|lon_deg=141|lon_min=0|lon_sec=11.6|label=Sumiyoshi Shrine|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|Alternativemap=Hokkaidō géolocalisation 000.svg}} | location within Hokkaido }} }} '''Sumiyoshi Shrine''' (住吉神社, ''Sumiyoshi Jinja'') is a [[Shinto]] shrine located in [[Otaru, Hokkaido]]. Its festival is on July 15.https://unga-plus.com/?pid=172674544 The [[kami]] Kamitsusu no O no Ōkami (上筒之男大神), Nakatsutsu no O no Ōkami (中筒之男大神), Sokotsutsu no O no Ōkami (底筒之男大神), Okinagaranushihime no Ōkami (息長足姫大神), Onamochi no Ōkami (大名持大神), [[Sukunabikona]] no Ōkami (少彦名大神) and others are enshrined here. ==Overview== {{Citation needed|date=March 2024}} '''Sumiyoshi Shrine''' (住吉神社, Sumiyoshi Jinja) is a Shinto shrine located in [[Otaru]], [[Hokkaido]], dedicated to the Sumiyoshi deities (Sokotutsuo no Kami, Nakatutsuo no Kami, Uwatsutsuo no Kami, and Okitsuhime no Mikoto). It is currently classified as a Beppyo Shrine under the [[Association of Shinto Shrines]], and its former rank under the modern shrine ranking system was Prefectural Shrine. == History == * [[1865]] (Keio 1): Shigeakira Kikuchi, a priest from Hakodate Hachiman Shrine, petitioned the Hakodate Bugyōsho to enshrine the Sumiyoshi deities as the general guardians for the Otarunai and Takashima areas. Permission was granted to enshrine them in Otarunai. * [[1868]] (Meiji 1): The sacred objects arrived in Otarunai. Due to the unprepared shrine grounds, they were temporarily enshrined at Itsukushima Shrine, where the consecration ceremony took place. * [[1875]] (Meiji 8): The shrine was classified as a Gōsha (village shrine). * [[1881]] (Meiji 14): The shrine buildings were constructed at the current location, and the shrine was relocated there. * [[1896]] (Meiji 29): The shrine was renamed from "Suminoe Shrine" to "Sumiyoshi Shrine". * [[1906]] (Meiji 39): The shrine was elevated to Prefectural Shrine status. * [[1955]] (Showa 30): The shrine was added to the list of Beppyo Shrines under the Association of Shinto Shrines. * [[1971]] (Showa 46): To commemorate the 100th anniversary of its establishment, the shrine buildings were renovated. * [[1997]] (Heisei 9): To mark the 130th anniversary, the shrine’s mikoshi (portable shrine), the largest in Hokkaido, was restored. * [[2007]] (Heisei 19): To celebrate the 140th anniversary, a mikoshi warehouse was built to house five mikoshi and one horse-drawn carriage. == annual Grand festival == The Sumiyoshi Shrine annual Grand festival, commonly known as the "Otaru festival," is considered one of the three major festivals in Otaru.{{Cite web | url = http://www.hokkaido-np.co.jp/news/chiiki/551341.html | title = 小樽・住吉神社例大祭 露店150店連なる 16日まで | work = [[Hokkaido Shimbun]] | publisher = Hokkaido Shimbunsha | date = 2014-07-15 | access-date = 2014-07-16 | archive-url = https://web.archive.org/web/20140726221521/https://www.hokkaido-np.co.jp/news/chiiki/551341.html | archive-date = 2014-07-26 }} == Access == * [[JR Hokkaido]] [[Hakodate Main Line]] [[Minami-Otaru Station]], an 8-minute walk * [[Hokkaido Chuo Bus]] (Otaru Depot, Shin Sapporo Depot, Satsuru Line), [[JR Hokkaido Bus]] (Satsuru Line), "Sumiyoshi Shrine Mae" bus stop, a 1-minute walk == Gallery == Sumiyoshi Shrine of Otaru - Shamusho.jpg|Office (Otaru City Designated Historical Building) Sumiyoshi Shrine of Otaru - Haiden.jpg|Worship Hall Kataro Matsukawa Permission was granted to enshrine them in Otarunai. * [[1868]] (Meiji 1): The sacred objects arrived in Otarunai. Due to the unprepared shrine grounds, they were temporarily enshrined at Itsukushima Shrine, where the consecration ceremony took place. * [[1875]] (Meiji 8): The shrine was classified as a Gōsha (village shrine). * [[1881]] (Meiji 14): The shrine buildings were constructed at the current location, and the shrine was relocated there. * [[1896]] (Meiji 29): The shrine was renamed from "Suminoe Shrine" to "Sumiyoshi Shrine". * [[1906]] (Meiji 39): The shrine was elevated to Prefectural Shrine status. * [[1955]] (Showa 30): The shrine was added to the list of Beppyo Shrines under the Association of Shinto Shrines. * [[1971]] (Showa 46): To commemorate the 100th anniversary of its establishment, the shrine buildings were renovated. * [[1997]] (Heisei 9): To mark the 130th anniversary, the shrine’s mikoshi (portable shrine), the largest in Hokkaido, was restored. * [[2007]] (Heisei 19): To celebrate the 140th anniversary, a mikoshi warehouse was built to house five mikoshi and one horse-drawn carriage. == annual Grand festival == The Sumiyoshi Shrine annual Grand festival, commonly known as the "Otaru festival," is considered one of the three major festivals in Otaru.{{Cite web | url = http://www.hokkaido-np.co.jp/news/chiiki/551341.html | title = 小樽・住吉神社例大祭 露店150店連なる 16日まで | work = [[Hokkaido Shimbun]] | publisher = Hokkaido Shimbunsha | date = 2014-07-15 | access-date = 2014-07-16 | archive-url = https://web.archive.org/web/20140726221521/https://www.hokkaido-np.co.jp/news/chiiki/551341.html | archive-date = 2014-07-26 }} == Access == * [[JR Hokkaido]] [[Torii]] | location = [[Hokkaido]][[Otaru]] Suminoye 2-5-1 | coordinates = {{coord|43|10|58.6|N|141|0|11.6|E|region:JP-01_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{Plainlist| * [[Sokotutsuo no Mikoto|Sokotutsuo no Kami]] * [[Nakatutsuo no Mikoto|Nakatutsuo no Kami]] * [[Uwatsutsuo no Mikoto|Uwatsutsuo no Kami]] * [[Empress Jingū|Okitsuhime no Mikoto]] }} | type = {{Plainlist| * Former Prefectural Shrine * Beppyo Shrine }} | founded = [[1868]] (Meiji 1) | main hall= [[Sumiyoshi-zukuri]] | festival = July 15 | Rites = {{Plainlist| * Taishakagura Dedication festival * annual Grand festival * Autumn Grand festival }} | map = {{Switcher | {{maplink2|zoom=14|frame=yes|plain=yes|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|type=shape|stroke-width=2}} | location within Otaru | {{location map|Japan Hokkaido|lat_deg=43|lat_min=10|lat_sec=58.6|lon_deg=141|lon_min=0|lon_sec=11.6|label=Sumiyoshi Shrine|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|Alternativemap=Hokkaidō géolocalisation 000.svg}} | location within Hokkaido }} }} '''Sumiyoshi Shrine''' (住吉神社, ''Sumiyoshi Jinja'') is a [[Shinto]] shrine located in [[Otaru, Hokkaido]]. Its festival is on July 15.https://unga-plus.com/?pid=172674544 The [[kami]] Kamitsusu no O no Ōkami (上筒之男大神), Nakatsutsu no O no Ōkami (中筒之男大神), Sokotsutsu no O no Ōkami (底筒之男大神), Okinagaranushihime no Ōkami (息長足姫大神), Onamochi no Ōkami (大名持大神), [[Sukunabikona]] no Ōkami (少彦名大神) and others are enshrined here. ==Overview== {{Citation needed|date=March 2024}} '''Sumiyoshi Shrine''' (住吉神社, Sumiyoshi Jinja) is a Shinto shrine located in [[Otaru]], [[Hokkaido]], dedicated to the Sumiyoshi deities (Sokotutsuo no Kami, Nakatutsuo no Kami, Uwatsutsuo no Kami, and Okitsuhime no Mikoto). It is currently classified as a Beppyo Shrine under the [[Association of Shinto Shrines]], and its former rank under the modern shrine ranking system was Prefectural Shrine. == History == * [[1865]] (Keio 1): Shigeakira Kikuchi, a priest from Hakodate Hachiman Shrine, petitioned the Hakodate Bugyōsho to enshrine the Sumiyoshi deities as the general guardians for the Otarunai and Takashima areas. Permission was granted to enshrine them in Otarunai. * [[1868]] (Meiji 1): The sacred objects arrived in Otarunai. Due to the unprepared shrine grounds, they were temporarily enshrined at Itsukushima Shrine, where the consecration ceremony took place. * [[1875]] (Meiji 8): The shrine was classified as a Gōsha (village shrine). * [[1881]] (Meiji 14): The shrine buildings were constructed at the current location, and the shrine was relocated there. * [[1896]] (Meiji 29): The shrine was renamed from "Suminoe Shrine" to "Sumiyoshi Shrine". * [[1906]] (Meiji 39): The shrine was elevated to Prefectural Shrine status. * [[1955]] (Showa 30): The shrine was added to the list of Beppyo Shrines under the Association of Shinto Shrines. * [[1971]] (Showa 46): To commemorate the 100th anniversary of its establishment, the shrine buildings were renovated. * [[1997]] (Heisei 9): To mark the 130th anniversary, the shrine’s mikoshi (portable shrine), the largest in Hokkaido, was restored. * [[2007]] (Heisei 19): To celebrate the 140th anniversary, a mikoshi warehouse was built to house five mikoshi and one horse-drawn carriage. == annual Grand festival == The Sumiyoshi Shrine annual Grand festival, commonly known as the "Otaru festival," is considered one of the three major festivals in Otaru.{{Cite web | url = http://www.hokkaido-np.co.jp/news/chiiki/551341.html | title = 小樽・住吉神社例大祭 露店150店連なる 16日まで | work = [[Hokkaido Shimbun]] | publisher = Hokkaido Shimbunsha | date = 2014-07-15 | access-date = 2014-07-16 | archive-url = https://web.archive.org/web/20140726221521/https://www.hokkaido-np.co.jp/news/chiiki/551341.html | archive-date = 2014-07-26 }} == Access == * [[JR Hokkaido]] [[Hakodate Main Line]] [[Minami-Otaru Station]], an 8-minute walk * [[Hokkaido Chuo Bus]] (Otaru Depot, Shin Sapporo Depot, Satsuru Line), [[JR Hokkaido Bus]] (Satsuru Line), "Sumiyoshi Shrine Mae" bus stop, a 1-minute walk == Gallery == Sumiyoshi Shrine of Otaru - Shamusho.jpg|Office (Otaru City Designated Historical Building) Sumiyoshi Shrine of Otaru - Haiden.jpg|Worship Hall Kataro Matsukawa 20210919.jpg|Statue of Katataro Matsukawa Monument to National Foundation Day at Sumiyoshi Shrine of Otaru.jpg|Monument to National Foundation Day Monument to Kitchen Knives at Sumiyoshi Shrine of Otaru.jpg|Kitchen Knife Monument Height Mesurement Stone at Sumiyoshi Shrine of Otaru.jpg|Height Measurement Stone and Child Blessing Dog File:第二次世界大戦前の住吉神社（北海道小樽市）.jpg|Sumiyoshi Shrine approach in the Taisho era (postcard dated July 9, 1919) == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat}} {{Osm box|w|665046858}} * {{Official website|url=http://www.otarusumiyoshijinja.or.jp/|name=Otaru General Guardian "Sumiyoshi Shrine"}} - Official website * [https://hokkaidojinjacho.jp/%e4%bd%8f%e5%90%89%e7%a5%9e%e7%a4%be-3/ Sumiyoshi Shrine] - Hokkaido Shrine Association {{-}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Sumiyoshi Shrine Otaru}} [[category:kk]][[category:Shinto shrines in Hokkaido]] [[category:kk]][[category:Sumiyoshi Shrines]] {{Short description|Shrine in Hokkaido, Japan}} {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Sumiyoshi Shrine | image = [[File:Sumiyoshi Shrine of Otaru.jpg|250px|Sumiyoshi Shrine]]
[[Torii]] | location = [[Hokkaido]][[Otaru]] Suminoye 2-5-1 | coordinates = {{coord|43|10|58.6|N|141|0|11.6|E|region:JP-01_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{Plainlist| * [[Sokotutsuo no Mikoto|Sokotutsuo no Kami]] * [[Nakatutsuo no Mikoto|Nakatutsuo no Kami]] * [[Uwatsutsuo no Mikoto|Uwatsutsuo no Kami]] * [[Empress Jingū|Okitsuhime no Mikoto]] }} | type = {{Plainlist| * Former Prefectural Shrine * Beppyo Shrine }} | founded = [[1868]] (Meiji 1) | main hall= [[Sumiyoshi-zukuri]] | festival = July 15 | Rites = {{Plainlist| * Taishakagura Dedication festival * annual Grand festival * Autumn Grand festival }} | map = {{Switcher | {{maplink2|zoom=14|frame=yes|plain=yes|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|type=shape|stroke-width=2}} | location within Otaru | {{location map|Japan Hokkaido|lat_deg=43|lat_min=10|lat_sec=58.6|lon_deg=141|lon_min=0|lon_sec=11.6|label=Sumiyoshi Shrine|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|Alternativemap=Hokkaidō géolocalisation 000.svg}} | location within Hokkaido }} }} '''Sumiyoshi Shrine''' (住吉神社, ''Sumiyoshi Jinja'') is a [[Shinto]] shrine located in [[Otaru, Hokkaido]]. Its festival is on July 15.https://unga-plus.com/?pid=172674544 The [[kami]] Kamitsusu no O no Ōkami (上筒之男大神), Nakatsutsu no O no Ōkami (中筒之男大神), Sokotsutsu no O no Ōkami (底筒之男大神), Okinagaranushihime no Ōkami (息長足姫大神), Onamochi no Ōkami (大名持大神), [[Sukunabikona]] no Ōkami (少彦名大神) and others are enshrined here. ==Overview== {{Citation needed|date=March 2024}} '''Sumiyoshi Shrine''' (住吉神社, Sumiyoshi Jinja) is a Shinto shrine located in [[Otaru]], [[Hokkaido]], dedicated to the Sumiyoshi deities (Sokotutsuo no Kami, Nakatutsuo no Kami, Uwatsutsuo no Kami, and Okitsuhime no Mikoto). It is currently classified as a Beppyo Shrine under the [[Association of Shinto Shrines]], and its former rank under the modern shrine ranking system was Prefectural Shrine. == History == * [[1865]] (Keio 1): Shigeakira Kikuchi, a priest from Hakodate Hachiman Shrine, petitioned the Hakodate Bugyōsho to enshrine the Sumiyoshi deities as the general guardians for the Otarunai and Takashima areas. Permission was granted to enshrine them in Otarunai. * [[1868]] (Meiji 1): The sacred objects arrived in Otarunai. Due to the unprepared shrine grounds, they were temporarily enshrined at Itsukushima Shrine, where the consecration ceremony took place. * [[1875]] (Meiji 8): The shrine was classified as a Gōsha (village shrine). * [[1881]] (Meiji 14): The shrine buildings were constructed at the current location, and the shrine was relocated there. * [[1896]] (Meiji 29): The shrine was renamed from "Suminoe Shrine" to "Sumiyoshi Shrine". * [[1906]] (Meiji 39): The shrine was elevated to Prefectural Shrine status. * [[1955]] (Showa 30): The shrine was added to the list of Beppyo Shrines under the Association of Shinto Shrines. * [[1971]] (Showa 46): To commemorate the 100th anniversary of its establishment, the shrine buildings were renovated. * [[1997]] (Heisei 9): To mark the 130th anniversary, the shrine’s mikoshi (portable shrine), the largest in Hokkaido, was restored. * [[2007]] (Heisei 19): To celebrate the 140th anniversary, a mikoshi warehouse was built to house five mikoshi and one horse-drawn carriage. == annual Grand festival == The Sumiyoshi Shrine annual Grand festival, commonly known as the "Otaru festival," is considered one of the three major festivals in Otaru.{{Cite web | url = http://www.hokkaido-np.co.jp/news/chiiki/551341.html | title = 小樽・住吉神社例大祭 露店150店連なる 16日まで | work = [[Hokkaido Shimbun]] | publisher = Hokkaido Shimbunsha | date = 2014-07-15 | access-date = 2014-07-16 | archive-url = https://web.archive.org/web/20140726221521/https://www.hokkaido-np.co.jp/news/chiiki/551341.html | archive-date = 2014-07-26 }} == Access == * [[JR Hokkaido]] [[Hakodate Main Line]] [[Minami-Otaru Station]], an 8-minute walk * [[Hokkaido Chuo Bus]] (Otaru Depot, Shin Sapporo Depot, Satsuru Line), [[JR Hokkaido Bus]] (Satsuru Line), "Sumiyoshi Shrine Mae" bus stop, a 1-minute walk == Gallery == Sumiyoshi Shrine of Otaru - Shamusho.jpg|Office (Otaru City Designated Historical Building) Sumiyoshi Shrine of Otaru - Haiden.jpg|Worship Hall Kataro Matsukawa 20210919.jpg|Statue of Katataro Matsukawa Monument to National Foundation Day at Sumiyoshi Shrine of Otaru.jpg|Monument to National Foundation Day Monument to Kitchen Knives at Sumiyoshi Shrine of Otaru.jpg|Kitchen Knife Monument Height Mesurement Stone at Sumiyoshi Shrine of Otaru.jpg|Height Measurement Stone and Child Blessing Dog File:第二次世界大戦前の住吉神社（北海道小樽市）.jpg|Sumiyoshi Shrine approach in the Taisho era (postcard dated July 9, 1919) == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat}} {{Osm box|w|665046858}} * {{Official website|url=http://www.otarusumiyoshijinja.or.jp/|name=Otaru General Guardian "Sumiyoshi Shrine"}} - Official website * [https://hokkaidojinjacho.jp/%e4%bd%8f%e5%90%89%e7%a5%9e%e7%a4%be-3/ Sumiyoshi Shrine] - Hokkaido Shrine Association {{-}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Sumiyoshi Shrine Otaru}} [[category:kk]][[category:Shinto shrines in Hokkaido]] [[category:kk]][[category:Sumiyoshi Shrines]] {{Short description|Shrine in Hokkaido, Japan}} {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Sumiyoshi Shrine | image = [[File:Sumiyoshi Shrine of Otaru.jpg|250px|Sumiyoshi Shrine]]
[[Torii]] | location = [[Hokkaido]][[Otaru]] Suminoye 2-5-1 | coordinates = {{coord|43|10|58.6|N|141|0|11.6|E|region:JP-01_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{Plainlist| * [[Sokotutsuo no Mikoto|Sokotutsuo no Kami]] * [[Nakatutsuo no Mikoto|Nakatutsuo no Kami]] * [[Uwatsutsuo no Mikoto|Uwatsutsuo no Kami]] * [[Empress Jingū|Okitsuhime no Mikoto]] }} | type = {{Plainlist| * Former Prefectural Shrine * Beppyo Shrine }} | founded = [[1868]] (Meiji 1) | main hall= [[Sumiyoshi-zukuri]] | festival = July 15 | Rites = {{Plainlist| * Taishakagura Dedication festival * annual Grand festival * Autumn Grand festival }} | map = {{Switcher | {{maplink2|zoom=14|frame=yes|plain=yes|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|type=shape|stroke-width=2}} | location within Otaru | {{location map|Japan Hokkaido|lat_deg=43|lat_min=10|lat_sec=58.6|lon_deg=141|lon_min=0|lon_sec=11.6|label=Sumiyoshi Shrine|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|Alternativemap=Hokkaidō géolocalisation 000.svg}} | location within Hokkaido }} }} '''Sumiyoshi Shrine''' (住吉神社, ''Sumiyoshi Jinja'') is a [[Shinto]] shrine located in [[Otaru, Hokkaido]]. Its festival is on July 15.https://unga-plus.com/?pid=172674544 The [[kami]] Kamitsusu no O no Ōkami (上筒之男大神), Nakatsutsu no O no Ōkami (中筒之男大神), Sokotsutsu no O no Ōkami (底筒之男大神), Okinagaranushihime no Ōkami (息長足姫大神), Onamochi no Ōkami (大名持大神), [[Sukunabikona]] no Ōkami (少彦名大神) and others are enshrined here. ==Overview== {{Citation needed|date=March 2024}} '''Sumiyoshi Shrine''' (住吉神社, Sumiyoshi Jinja) is a Shinto shrine located in [[Otaru]], [[Hokkaido]], dedicated to the Sumiyoshi deities (Sokotutsuo no Kami, Nakatutsuo no Kami, Uwatsutsuo no Kami, and Okitsuhime no Mikoto). It is currently classified as a Beppyo Shrine under the [[Association of Shinto Shrines]], and its former rank under the modern shrine ranking system was Prefectural Shrine. == History == * [[1865]] (Keio 1): Shigeakira Kikuchi, a priest from Hakodate Hachiman Shrine, petitioned the Hakodate Bugyōsho to enshrine the Sumiyoshi deities as the general guardians for the Otarunai and Takashima areas. Permission was granted to enshrine them in Otarunai. * [[1868]] (Meiji 1): The sacred objects arrived in Otarunai. Due to the unprepared shrine grounds, they were temporarily enshrined at Itsukushima Shrine, where the consecration ceremony took place. * [[1875]] (Meiji 8): The shrine was classified as a Gōsha (village shrine). * [[1881]] (Meiji 14): The shrine buildings were constructed at the current location, and the shrine was relocated there. * [[1896]] (Meiji 29): The shrine was renamed from "Suminoe Shrine" to "Sumiyoshi Shrine". * [[1906]] (Meiji 39): The shrine was elevated to Prefectural Shrine status. * [[1955]] (Showa 30): The shrine was added to the list of Beppyo Shrines under the Association of Shinto Shrines. * [[1971]] (Showa 46): To commemorate the 100th anniversary of its establishment, the shrine buildings were renovated. * [[1997]] (Heisei 9): To mark the 130th anniversary, the shrine’s mikoshi (portable shrine), the largest in Hokkaido, was restored. * [[2007]] (Heisei 19): To celebrate the 140th anniversary, a mikoshi warehouse was built to house five mikoshi and one horse-drawn carriage. == annual Grand festival == The Sumiyoshi Shrine annual Grand festival, commonly known as the "Otaru festival," is considered one of the three major festivals in Otaru.{{Cite web | url = http://www.hokkaido-np.co.jp/news/chiiki/551341.html | title = 小樽・住吉神社例大祭 露店150店連なる 16日まで | work = [[Hokkaido Shimbun]] | publisher = Hokkaido Shimbunsha | date = 2014-07-15 | access-date = 2014-07-16 | archive-url = https://web.archive.org/web/20140726221521/https://www.hokkaido-np.co.jp/news/chiiki/551341.html | archive-date = 2014-07-26 }} == Access == * [[JR Hokkaido]] [[Hakodate Main Line]] [[Minami-Otaru Station]], an 8-minute walk * [[Hokkaido Chuo Bus]] (Otaru Depot, Shin Sapporo Depot, Satsuru Line), [[JR Hokkaido Bus]] (Satsuru Line), "Sumiyoshi Shrine Mae" bus stop, a 1-minute walk == Gallery == Sumiyoshi Shrine of Otaru - Shamusho.jpg|Office (Otaru City Designated Historical Building) Sumiyoshi Shrine of Otaru - Haiden.jpg|Worship Hall Kataro Matsukawa Its festival is on July 15.https://unga-plus.com/?pid=172674544 The [[kami]] Kamitsusu no O no Ōkami (上筒之男大神), Nakatsutsu no O no Ōkami (中筒之男大神), Sokotsutsu no O no Ōkami (底筒之男大神), Okinagaranushihime no Ōkami (息長足姫大神), Onamochi no Ōkami (大名持大神), [[Sukunabikona]] no Ōkami (少彦名大神) and others are enshrined here. ==Overview== {{Citation needed|date=March 2024}} '''Sumiyoshi Shrine''' (住吉神社, Sumiyoshi Jinja) is a Shinto shrine located in [[Otaru]], [[Hokkaido]], dedicated to the Sumiyoshi deities (Sokotutsuo no Kami, Nakatutsuo no Kami, Uwatsutsuo no Kami, and Okitsuhime no Mikoto). It is currently classified as a Beppyo Shrine under the [[Association of Shinto Shrines]], and its former rank under the modern shrine ranking system was Prefectural Shrine. == History == * [[1865]] (Keio 1): Shigeakira Kikuchi, a priest from Hakodate Hachiman Shrine, petitioned the Hakodate Bugyōsho to enshrine the Sumiyoshi deities as the general guardians for the Otarunai and Takashima areas. Permission was granted to enshrine them in Otarunai. * [[1868]] (Meiji 1): The sacred objects arrived in Otarunai. Due to the unprepared shrine grounds, they were temporarily enshrined at Itsukushima Shrine, where the consecration ceremony took place. * [[1875]] (Meiji 8): The shrine was classified as a Gōsha (village shrine). * [[1881]] (Meiji 14): The shrine buildings were constructed at the current location, and the shrine was relocated there. * [[1896]] (Meiji 29): The shrine was renamed from "Suminoe Shrine" to "Sumiyoshi Shrine". * [[1906]] (Meiji 39): The shrine was elevated to Prefectural Shrine status. * [[1955]] (Showa 30): The shrine was added to the list of Beppyo Shrines under the Association of Shinto Shrines. * [[1971]] (Showa 46): To commemorate the 100th anniversary of its establishment, the shrine buildings were renovated. * [[1997]] (Heisei 9): To mark the 130th anniversary, the shrine’s mikoshi (portable shrine), the largest in Hokkaido, was restored. * [[2007]] (Heisei 19): To celebrate the 140th anniversary, a mikoshi warehouse was built to house five mikoshi and one horse-drawn carriage. == annual Grand festival == The Sumiyoshi Shrine annual Grand festival, commonly known as the "Otaru festival," is considered one of the three major festivals in Otaru.{{Cite web | url = http://www.hokkaido-np.co.jp/news/chiiki/551341.html | title = 小樽・住吉神社例大祭 露店150店連なる 16日まで | work = [[Hokkaido Shimbun]] | publisher = Hokkaido Shimbunsha | date = 2014-07-15 | access-date = 2014-07-16 | archive-url = https://web.archive.org/web/20140726221521/https://www.hokkaido-np.co.jp/news/chiiki/551341.html | archive-date = 2014-07-26 }} == Access == * [[JR Hokkaido]] [[Hakodate Main Line]] [[Minami-Otaru Station]], an 8-minute walk * [[Hokkaido Chuo Bus]] (Otaru Depot, Shin Sapporo Depot, Satsuru Line), [[JR Hokkaido Bus]] (Satsuru Line), "Sumiyoshi Shrine Mae" bus stop, a 1-minute walk == Gallery == Sumiyoshi Shrine of Otaru - Shamusho.jpg|Office (Otaru City Designated Historical Building) Sumiyoshi Shrine of Otaru - Haiden.jpg|Worship Hall Kataro Matsukawa 20210919.jpg|Statue of Katataro Matsukawa Monument to National Foundation Day at Sumiyoshi Shrine of Otaru.jpg|Monument to National Foundation Day Monument to Kitchen Knives at Sumiyoshi Shrine of Otaru.jpg|Kitchen Knife Monument Height Mesurement Stone at Sumiyoshi Shrine of Otaru.jpg|Height Measurement Stone and Child Blessing Dog File:第二次世界大戦前の住吉神社（北海道小樽市）.jpg|Sumiyoshi Shrine approach in the Taisho era (postcard dated July 9, 1919) == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat}} {{Osm box|w|665046858}} * {{Official
