{{Short description|Shrine in Hokkaido, Japan}} {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Sumiyoshi Shrine | image = [[File:Sumiyoshi Shrine of Otaru.jpg|250px|Sumiyoshi Shrine]]
[[Torii]] | location = [[Hokkaido]][[Otaru]] Suminoye 2-5-1 | coordinates = {{coord|43|10|58.6|N|141|0|11.6|E|region:JP-01_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{Plainlist| * [[Sokotutsuo no Mikoto|Sokotutsuo no Kami]] * [[Nakatutsuo no Mikoto|Nakatutsuo no Kami]] * [[Uwatsutsuo no Mikoto|Uwatsutsuo no Kami]] * [[Empress Jingū|Okitsuhime no Mikoto]] }} | type = {{Plainlist| * Former Prefectural Shrine * Beppyo Shrine }} | founded = [[1868]] (Meiji 1) | main hall= [[Sumiyoshi-zukuri]] | festival = July 15 | Rites = {{Plainlist| * Taishakagura Dedication festival * annual Grand festival * Autumn Grand festival }} | map = {{Switcher | {{maplink2|zoom=14|frame=yes|plain=yes|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|type=shape|stroke-width=2}} | location within Otaru | {{location map|Japan Hokkaido|lat_deg=43|lat_min=10|lat_sec=58.6|lon_deg=141|lon_min=0|lon_sec=11.6|label=Sumiyoshi Shrine|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|Alternativemap=Hokkaidō géolocalisation 000.svg}} | location within Hokkaido }} }} '''Sumiyoshi Shrine''' (住吉神社, ''Sumiyoshi Jinja'') is a [[Shinto]] shrine located in [[Otaru, Hokkaido]]. Its festival is on July 15.https://unga-plus.com/?pid=172674544 The [[kami]] Kamitsusu no O no Ōkami (上筒之男大神), Nakatsutsu no O no Ōkami (中筒之男大神), Sokotsutsu no O no Ōkami (底筒之男大神), Okinagaranushihime no Ōkami (息長足姫大神), Onamochi no Ōkami (大名持大神), [[Sukunabikona]] no Ōkami (少彦名大神) and others are enshrined here. ==Overview== {{Citation needed|date=March 2024}} '''Sumiyoshi Shrine''' (住吉神社, Sumiyoshi Jinja) is a Shinto shrine located in [[Otaru]], [[Hokkaido]], dedicated to the Sumiyoshi deities (Sokotutsuo no Kami, Nakatutsuo no Kami, Uwatsutsuo no Kami, and Okitsuhime no Mikoto). It is currently classified as a Beppyo Shrine under the [[Association of Shinto Shrines]], and its former rank under the modern shrine ranking system was Prefectural Shrine. == History == * [[1865]] (Keio 1): Shigeakira Kikuchi, a priest from Hakodate Hachiman Shrine, petitioned the Hakodate Bugyōsho to enshrine the Sumiyoshi deities as the general guardians for the Otarunai and Takashima areas. Permission was granted to enshrine them in Otarunai. * [[1868]] (Meiji 1): The sacred objects arrived in Otarunai. Due to the unprepared shrine grounds, they were temporarily enshrined at Itsukushima Shrine, where the consecration ceremony took place. * [[1875]] (Meiji 8): The shrine was classified as a Gōsha (village shrine). * [[1881]] (Meiji 14): The shrine buildings were constructed at the current location, and the shrine was relocated there. * [[1896]] (Meiji 29): The shrine was renamed from "Suminoe Shrine" to "Sumiyoshi Shrine". * [[1906]] (Meiji 39): The shrine was elevated to Prefectural Shrine status. * [[1955]] (Showa 30): The shrine was added to the list of Beppyo Shrines under the Association of Shinto Shrines. * [[1971]] (Showa 46): To commemorate the 100th anniversary of its establishment, the shrine buildings were renovated. * [[1997]] (Heisei 9): To mark the 130th anniversary, the shrine’s mikoshi (portable shrine), the largest in Hokkaido, was restored. * [[2007]] (Heisei 19): To celebrate the 140th anniversary, a mikoshi warehouse was built to house five mikoshi and one horse-drawn carriage. == annual Grand festival == The Sumiyoshi Shrine annual Grand festival, commonly known as the "Otaru festival," is considered one of the three major festivals in Otaru.{{Cite web | url = http://www.hokkaido-np.co.jp/news/chiiki/551341.html | title = 小樽・住吉神社例大祭 露店150店連なる 16日まで | work = [[Hokkaido Shimbun]] | publisher = Hokkaido Shimbunsha | date = 2014-07-15 | access-date = 2014-07-16 | archive-url = https://web.archive.org/web/20140726221521/https://www.hokkaido-np.co.jp/news/chiiki/551341.html | archive-date = 2014-07-26 }} == Access == * [[JR Hokkaido]] [[Hakodate Main Line]] [[Minami-Otaru Station]], an 8-minute walk * [[Hokkaido Chuo Bus]] (Otaru Depot, Shin Sapporo Depot, Satsuru Line), [[JR Hokkaido Bus]] (Satsuru Line), "Sumiyoshi Shrine Mae" bus stop, a 1-minute walk == Gallery == Sumiyoshi Shrine of Otaru - Shamusho.jpg|Office (Otaru City Designated Historical Building) Sumiyoshi Shrine of Otaru - Haiden.jpg|Worship Hall Kataro Matsukawa 20210919.jpg|Statue of Katataro Matsukawa Monument to National Foundation Day at Sumiyoshi Shrine of Otaru.jpg|Monument to National Foundation Day Monument to Kitchen Knives at Sumiyoshi Shrine of Shrine" to "Sumiyoshi Shrine". * [[1906]] (Meiji 39): The shrine was elevated to Prefectural Shrine status. * [[1955]] (Showa 30): The shrine was added to the list of Beppyo Shrines under the Association of Shinto Shrines. * [[1971]] (Showa 46): To commemorate the 100th anniversary of its establishment, the shrine buildings were renovated. * [[1997]] (Heisei 9): To mark the 130th anniversary, the shrine’s mikoshi (portable shrine), the largest in Hokkaido, was restored. * [[2007]] (Heisei 19): To celebrate the 140th anniversary, a mikoshi warehouse was built to house five mikoshi and one horse-drawn carriage. == annual Grand festival == The Sumiyoshi Shrine annual Grand festival, commonly known as the "Otaru festival," is considered one of the three major festivals in {{Short description|Shrine in Hokkaido, Japan}} {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Sumiyoshi Shrine | image = [[File:Sumiyoshi Shrine of Otaru.jpg|250px|Sumiyoshi Shrine]]
[[Torii]] | location = [[Hokkaido]][[Otaru]] Suminoye 2-5-1 | coordinates = {{coord|43|10|58.6|N|141|0|11.6|E|region:JP-01_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{Plainlist| * [[Sokotutsuo no Mikoto|Sokotutsuo no Kami]] * [[Nakatutsuo no Mikoto|Nakatutsuo no Kami]] * [[Uwatsutsuo no Mikoto|Uwatsutsuo no Kami]] * [[Empress Jingū|Okitsuhime no Mikoto]] }} | type = {{Plainlist| * Former Prefectural Shrine * Beppyo Shrine }} | founded = [[1868]] (Meiji 1) | main hall= [[Sumiyoshi-zukuri]] | festival = July 15 | Rites = {{Plainlist| * Taishakagura Dedication festival * annual Grand festival * Autumn Grand festival }} | map = {{Switcher | {{maplink2|zoom=14|frame=yes|plain=yes|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|type=shape|stroke-width=2}} | location within Otaru | {{location map|Japan Hokkaido|lat_deg=43|lat_min=10|lat_sec=58.6|lon_deg=141|lon_min=0|lon_sec=11.6|label=Sumiyoshi Shrine|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|Alternativemap=Hokkaidō géolocalisation 000.svg}} | location within Hokkaido }} }} '''Sumiyoshi Shrine''' (住吉神社, ''Sumiyoshi Jinja'') is a [[Shinto]] shrine located in [[Otaru, Hokkaido]]. Its festival is on July 15.https://unga-plus.com/?pid=172674544 The [[kami]] Kamitsusu no O no Ōkami (上筒之男大神), Nakatsutsu no O no Ōkami (中筒之男大神), Sokotsutsu no O no Ōkami (底筒之男大神), Okinagaranushihime no Ōkami (息長足姫大神), Onamochi no Ōkami (大名持大神), [[Sukunabikona]] no Ōkami (少彦名大神) and others are enshrined here. ==Overview== {{Citation needed|date=March 2024}} '''Sumiyoshi Shrine''' (住吉神社, Sumiyoshi Jinja) is a Shinto shrine located in [[Otaru]], [[Hokkaido]], dedicated to the Sumiyoshi deities (Sokotutsuo no Kami, Nakatutsuo no Kami, Uwatsutsuo no Kami, and Okitsuhime no Mikoto). It is currently classified as a Beppyo Shrine under the [[Association of Shinto Shrines]], and its former rank under the modern shrine ranking system was Prefectural Shrine. == History == * [[1865]] (Keio 1): Shigeakira Kikuchi, a priest from Hakodate Hachiman Shrine, petitioned the Hakodate Bugyōsho to enshrine the Sumiyoshi deities as the general guardians for the Otarunai and Takashima areas. Permission was granted to enshrine them in Otarunai. * [[1868]] (Meiji 1): The sacred objects arrived in Otarunai. Due to the unprepared shrine grounds, they were temporarily enshrined at Itsukushima Shrine, where the consecration ceremony took place. * [[1875]] (Meiji 8): The shrine was classified as a Gōsha (village shrine). * [[1881]] (Meiji 14): The shrine buildings were constructed at the current location, and the shrine was relocated there. * [[1896]] (Meiji 29): The shrine was renamed from "Suminoe Shrine" to "Sumiyoshi Shrine". * [[1906]] (Meiji 39): The shrine was elevated to Prefectural Shrine status. * [[1955]] (Showa 30): The shrine was added to the list of Beppyo Shrines under the Association of Shinto Shrines. * [[1971]] (Showa 46): To commemorate the 100th anniversary of its establishment, the shrine buildings were renovated. * [[1997]] (Heisei 9): To mark the 130th anniversary, the shrine’s mikoshi (portable shrine), the largest in Hokkaido, was restored. * [[2007]] (Heisei 19): To celebrate the 140th anniversary, a mikoshi warehouse was built to house five mikoshi and one horse-drawn carriage. == annual Grand festival == The Sumiyoshi Shrine annual Grand festival, commonly known as the "Otaru festival," is considered one of the three major festivals in Otaru.{{Cite web | url = http://www.hokkaido-np.co.jp/news/chiiki/551341.html | title = 小樽・住吉神社例大祭 露店150店連なる 16日まで | work = [[Hokkaido Shimbun]] | publisher = Hokkaido Shimbunsha | date = 2014-07-15 | access-date = 2014-07-16 | archive-url = https://web.archive.org/web/20140726221521/https://www.hokkaido-np.co.jp/news/chiiki/551341.html | archive-date = 2014-07-26 }} == Access == * [[JR Hokkaido]] [[Hakodate Main Line]] [[Minami-Otaru Station]], an 8-minute walk * [[Hokkaido Chuo Bus]] (Otaru Depot, Shin Sapporo Depot, Satsuru Line), [[JR Hokkaido Bus]] (Satsuru Line), "Sumiyoshi Shrine Mae" bus stop, a 1-minute walk == Gallery == Sumiyoshi Shrine of Otaru - Shamusho.jpg|Office (Otaru City Designated Historical Building) Sumiyoshi Shrine of Otaru - Haiden.jpg|Worship Hall Kataro Matsukawa {{Short description|Shrine in Hokkaido, Japan}} {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Sumiyoshi Shrine | image = [[File:Sumiyoshi Shrine of Otaru.jpg|250px|Sumiyoshi Shrine]]
[[Torii]] | location = [[Hokkaido]][[Otaru]] Suminoye 2-5-1 | coordinates = {{coord|43|10|58.6|N|141|0|11.6|E|region:JP-01_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{Plainlist| * [[Sokotutsuo no Mikoto|Sokotutsuo no Kami]] * [[Nakatutsuo no Mikoto|Nakatutsuo no Kami]] * [[Uwatsutsuo no Mikoto|Uwatsutsuo no Kami]] * [[Empress Jingū|Okitsuhime no Mikoto]] }} | type = {{Plainlist| * Former Prefectural Shrine * Beppyo Shrine }} | founded = [[1868]] (Meiji 1) | main hall= [[Sumiyoshi-zukuri]] | festival = July 15 | Rites = {{Plainlist| * Taishakagura Dedication festival * annual Grand festival * Autumn Grand festival }} | map = {{Switcher | {{maplink2|zoom=14|frame=yes|plain=yes|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|type=shape|stroke-width=2}} | location within Otaru | {{location map|Japan Hokkaido|lat_deg=43|lat_min=10|lat_sec=58.6|lon_deg=141|lon_min=0|lon_sec=11.6|label=Sumiyoshi Shrine|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|Alternativemap=Hokkaidō géolocalisation 000.svg}} | location within Hokkaido }} }} '''Sumiyoshi Shrine''' (住吉神社, ''Sumiyoshi Jinja'') is a [[Shinto]] shrine located in [[Otaru, Hokkaido]]. Its festival is on July 15.https://unga-plus.com/?pid=172674544 The [[kami]] Kamitsusu no O no Ōkami (上筒之男大神), Nakatsutsu no O no Ōkami (中筒之男大神), Sokotsutsu no O no Ōkami (底筒之男大神), Okinagaranushihime no Ōkami (息長足姫大神), Onamochi no Ōkami (大名持大神), [[Sukunabikona]] no Ōkami (少彦名大神) and others are enshrined here. ==Overview== {{Citation needed|date=March 2024}} '''Sumiyoshi Shrine''' (住吉神社, Sumiyoshi Jinja) is a Shinto shrine located in [[Otaru]], [[Hokkaido]], dedicated to the Sumiyoshi deities (Sokotutsuo no Kami, Nakatutsuo no Kami, Uwatsutsuo no Kami, and Okitsuhime no Mikoto). It is currently classified as a Beppyo Shrine under the [[Association of Shinto Shrines]], and its former rank under the modern shrine ranking system was Prefectural Shrine. == History == * [[1865]] (Keio 1): Shigeakira Kikuchi, a priest from Hakodate Hachiman Shrine, petitioned the Hakodate Bugyōsho to enshrine the Sumiyoshi deities as the general guardians for the Otarunai and Takashima areas. Permission was granted to enshrine them in Otarunai. * [[1868]] (Meiji 1): The sacred objects arrived in Otarunai. Due to the unprepared shrine grounds, they were temporarily enshrined at Itsukushima Shrine, where the consecration ceremony took place. * [[1875]] (Meiji 8): The shrine was classified as a Gōsha (village shrine). * [[1881]] (Meiji 14): The shrine buildings were constructed at the current location, and the shrine was relocated there. * [[1896]] (Meiji 29): The shrine was renamed from "Suminoe Shrine" to "Sumiyoshi Shrine". * [[1906]] (Meiji 39): The shrine was elevated to Prefectural Shrine status. * [[1955]] (Showa 30): The shrine was added to the list of Beppyo Shrines under the Association of Shinto Shrines. * [[1971]] (Showa 46): To commemorate the 100th anniversary of its establishment, the shrine buildings were renovated. * [[1997]] (Heisei 9): To mark the 130th anniversary, the shrine’s mikoshi (portable shrine), the largest in Hokkaido, was restored. * [[2007]] (Heisei 19): To celebrate the 140th anniversary, a mikoshi warehouse was built to house five mikoshi and one horse-drawn carriage. == annual Grand festival == The Sumiyoshi Shrine annual Grand festival, commonly known as the "Otaru festival," is considered one of the three major festivals in Otaru.{{Cite web | url = http://www.hokkaido-np.co.jp/news/chiiki/551341.html | title = 小樽・住吉神社例大祭 露店150店連なる 16日まで | work = [[Hokkaido Shimbun]] | publisher = Hokkaido Shimbunsha | date = 2014-07-15 | access-date = 2014-07-16 | archive-url = https://web.archive.org/web/20140726221521/https://www.hokkaido-np.co.jp/news/chiiki/551341.html | archive-date = 2014-07-26 }} == Access == * [[JR Hokkaido]] [[Hakodate Main Line]] [[Minami-Otaru Station]], an 8-minute walk * [[Hokkaido Chuo Bus]] (Otaru Depot, Shin Sapporo Depot, Satsuru Line), [[JR Hokkaido Bus]] (Satsuru Line), "Sumiyoshi Shrine Mae" bus stop, a 1-minute walk == Gallery == Sumiyoshi Shrine of Otaru - Shamusho.jpg|Office (Otaru City Designated Historical Building) Sumiyoshi Shrine of Otaru - Haiden.jpg|Worship Hall Kataro Matsukawa 20210919.jpg|Statue of Katataro Matsukawa Monument to National Foundation Day at Sumiyoshi Shrine of Otaru.jpg|Monument to National Foundation Day Monument to Kitchen Knives at Sumiyoshi Shrine of {{Short description|Shrine in Hokkaido, Japan}} {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Sumiyoshi Shrine | image = [[File:Sumiyoshi Shrine of Otaru.jpg|250px|Sumiyoshi Shrine]]
[[Torii]] | location = [[Hokkaido]][[Otaru]] Suminoye 2-5-1 | coordinates = {{coord|43|10|58.6|N|141|0|11.6|E|region:JP-01_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{Plainlist| * [[Sokotutsuo no Mikoto|Sokotutsuo no Kami]] * [[Nakatutsuo no Mikoto|Nakatutsuo no Kami]] * [[Uwatsutsuo no Mikoto|Uwatsutsuo no Kami]] * [[Empress Jingū|Okitsuhime no Mikoto]] }} | type = {{Plainlist| * Former Prefectural Shrine * Beppyo Shrine }} | founded = [[1868]] (Meiji 1) | main hall= [[Sumiyoshi-zukuri]] | festival = July 15 | Rites = {{Plainlist| * Taishakagura Dedication festival * annual Grand festival * Autumn Grand festival }} | map = {{Switcher | {{maplink2|zoom=14|frame=yes|plain=yes|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|type=shape|stroke-width=2}} | location within Otaru | {{location map|Japan Hokkaido|lat_deg=43|lat_min=10|lat_sec=58.6|lon_deg=141|lon_min=0|lon_sec=11.6|label=Sumiyoshi Shrine|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|Alternativemap=Hokkaidō géolocalisation 000.svg}} | location within Hokkaido }} }} '''Sumiyoshi Shrine''' (住吉神社, ''Sumiyoshi Jinja'') is a [[Shinto]] shrine located in [[Otaru, Hokkaido]]. Its festival is on July 15.https://unga-plus.com/?pid=172674544 The [[kami]] Kamitsusu no O no Ōkami (上筒之男大神), Nakatsutsu no O no Ōkami (中筒之男大神), Sokotsutsu no O no Ōkami (底筒之男大神), Okinagaranushihime no Ōkami (息長足姫大神), Onamochi no Ōkami (大名持大神), [[Sukunabikona]] no Ōkami (少彦名大神) and others are enshrined here. ==Overview== {{Citation needed|date=March 2024}} '''Sumiyoshi Shrine''' (住吉神社, Sumiyoshi Jinja) is a Shinto shrine located in [[Otaru]], [[Hokkaido]], dedicated to the Sumiyoshi deities (Sokotutsuo no Kami, Nakatutsuo no Kami, Uwatsutsuo no Kami, and Okitsuhime no Mikoto). It is currently classified as a Beppyo Shrine under the [[Association of Shinto Shrines]], and its former rank under the modern shrine ranking system was Prefectural Shrine. == History == * [[1865]] (Keio 1): Shigeakira Kikuchi, a priest from Hakodate Hachiman Shrine, petitioned the Hakodate Bugyōsho to enshrine the Sumiyoshi deities as the general guardians for the Otarunai and Takashima areas. Permission was granted to enshrine them in Otarunai. * [[1868]] (Meiji 1): The sacred objects arrived in Otarunai. Due to the unprepared shrine grounds, they were temporarily enshrined at Itsukushima Shrine, where the consecration ceremony took place. * [[1875]] (Meiji 8): The shrine was classified as a Gōsha (village shrine). * [[1881]] (Meiji 14): The shrine buildings were constructed at the current location, and the shrine was relocated there. * [[1896]] (Meiji 29): The shrine was renamed from "Suminoe Shrine" to "Sumiyoshi Shrine". * [[1906]] (Meiji 39): The shrine was elevated to Prefectural Shrine status. * [[1955]] (Showa 30): The shrine was added to the list of Beppyo Shrines under the Association of Shinto Shrines. * [[1971]] (Showa 46): To commemorate the 100th anniversary of its establishment, the shrine buildings were renovated. * [[1997]] (Heisei 9): To mark the 130th anniversary, the shrine’s mikoshi (portable shrine), the largest in Hokkaido, was restored. * [[2007]] (Heisei 19): To celebrate the 140th anniversary, a mikoshi warehouse was built to house five mikoshi and one horse-drawn carriage. == annual Grand festival == The Sumiyoshi Shrine annual Grand festival, commonly known as the "Otaru festival," is considered one of the three major festivals in Otaru.{{Cite web | url = http://www.hokkaido-np.co.jp/news/chiiki/551341.html | title = 小樽・住吉神社例大祭 露店150店連なる 16日まで | work = [[Hokkaido Shimbun]] | publisher = Hokkaido Shimbunsha | date = 2014-07-15 | access-date = 2014-07-16 | archive-url = https://web.archive.org/web/20140726221521/https://www.hokkaido-np.co.jp/news/chiiki/551341.html | archive-date = 2014-07-26 }} == Access == * [[JR Hokkaido]] [[Hakodate Main Line]] [[Minami-Otaru Station]], an 8-minute walk * [[Hokkaido Chuo Bus]] (Otaru Depot, Shin Sapporo Depot, Satsuru Line), [[JR Hokkaido Bus]] (Satsuru Line), "Sumiyoshi Shrine Mae" bus stop, a 1-minute walk == Gallery == Sumiyoshi Shrine of Otaru - Shamusho.jpg|Office (Otaru City Designated Historical Building) Sumiyoshi Shrine of Otaru - Haiden.jpg|Worship Hall Kataro Matsukawa Its festival is on July 15.https://unga-plus.com/?pid=172674544 The [[kami]] Kamitsusu no O no Ōkami (上筒之男大神), Nakatsutsu no O no Ōkami (中筒之男大神), Sokotsutsu no O no Ōkami (底筒之男大神), Okinagaranushihime no Ōkami (息長足姫大神), Onamochi no Ōkami (大名持大神), [[Sukunabikona]] no Ōkami (少彦名大神) and others are enshrined here. ==Overview== {{Citation needed|date=March 2024}} '''Sumiyoshi Shrine''' (住吉神社, Sumiyoshi Jinja) is a Shinto shrine located in [[Otaru]], [[Hokkaido]], dedicated to the Sumiyoshi deities (Sokotutsuo no Kami, Nakatutsuo no Kami, Uwatsutsuo no Kami, and Okitsuhime no Mikoto). It is currently classified as a Beppyo Shrine under the [[Association of Shinto Shrines]], and its former rank under the modern shrine ranking system was Prefectural Shrine. == History == * [[1865]] (Keio 1): Shigeakira Kikuchi, a priest from Hakodate Hachiman Shrine, petitioned the Hakodate Bugyōsho to enshrine the Sumiyoshi deities as the general guardians for the Otarunai and Takashima areas. Permission was granted to enshrine them in Otarunai. * [[1868]] (Meiji 1): The sacred objects arrived in Otarunai. Due to the unprepared shrine grounds, they were temporarily enshrined at Itsukushima Shrine, where the consecration ceremony took place. * [[1875]] (Meiji 8): The shrine was classified as a Gōsha (village shrine). * [[1881]] (Meiji 14): The shrine buildings were constructed at the current location, and the shrine was relocated there. * [[1896]] (Meiji 29): The shrine was renamed from "Suminoe Shrine" to "Sumiyoshi Shrine". * [[1906]] (Meiji 39): The shrine was elevated to Prefectural Shrine status. * [[1955]] (Showa 30): The shrine was added to the list of Beppyo Shrines under the Association of Shinto Shrines. * [[1971]] (Showa 46): To commemorate the 100th anniversary of its establishment, the shrine buildings were renovated. * [[1997]] (Heisei 9): To mark the 130th anniversary, the shrine’s mikoshi (portable shrine), the largest in Hokkaido, was restored. * [[2007]] (Heisei 19): To celebrate the 140th anniversary, a mikoshi warehouse was built to house five mikoshi and one horse-drawn carriage. == annual Grand festival == The Sumiyoshi Shrine annual Grand festival, commonly known as the "Otaru festival," is considered one of the three major festivals in Otaru.{{Cite web | url = http://www.hokkaido-np.co.jp/news/chiiki/551341.html | title = 小樽・住吉神社例大祭 露店150店連なる 16日まで | work = [[Hokkaido Shimbun]] | publisher = Hokkaido Shimbunsha | date = 2014-07-15 | access-date = 2014-07-16 | archive-url = https://web.archive.org/web/20140726221521/https://www.hokkaido-np.co.jp/news/chiiki/551341.html | archive-date = 2014-07-26 }} == Access == * [[JR Hokkaido]] bus stop, a 1-minute walk == Gallery == Sumiyoshi Shrine of Otaru - Shamusho.jpg|Office (Otaru City Designated Historical Building) Sumiyoshi Shrine of Otaru - Haiden.jpg|Worship Hall Kataro Matsukawa {{Short description|Shrine in Hokkaido, Japan}} {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Sumiyoshi Shrine | image = [[File:Sumiyoshi Shrine of Otaru.jpg|250px|Sumiyoshi Shrine]]
[[Torii]] | location = [[Hokkaido]][[Otaru]] Suminoye 2-5-1 | coordinates = {{coord|43|10|58.6|N|141|0|11.6|E|region:JP-01_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{Plainlist| * [[Sokotutsuo no Mikoto|Sokotutsuo no Kami]] * [[Nakatutsuo no Mikoto|Nakatutsuo no Kami]] * [[Uwatsutsuo no Mikoto|Uwatsutsuo no Kami]] * [[Empress Jingū|Okitsuhime no Mikoto]] }} | type = {{Plainlist| * Former Prefectural Shrine * Beppyo Shrine }} | founded = [[1868]] (Meiji 1) | main hall= [[Sumiyoshi-zukuri]] | festival = July 15 | Rites = {{Plainlist| * Taishakagura Dedication festival * annual Grand festival * Autumn Grand festival }} | map = {{Switcher | {{maplink2|zoom=14|frame=yes|plain=yes|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|type=shape|stroke-width=2}} | location within Otaru | {{location map|Japan Hokkaido|lat_deg=43|lat_min=10|lat_sec=58.6|lon_deg=141|lon_min=0|lon_sec=11.6|label=Sumiyoshi Shrine|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|Alternativemap=Hokkaidō géolocalisation 000.svg}} | location within Hokkaido }} }} '''Sumiyoshi Shrine''' (住吉神社, ''Sumiyoshi Jinja'') is a [[Shinto]] shrine located in [[Otaru, Hokkaido]]. Its festival is on July 15.https://unga-plus.com/?pid=172674544 The [[kami]] Kamitsusu no O no Ōkami (上筒之男大神), Nakatsutsu no O no Ōkami (中筒之男大神), Sokotsutsu no O no Ōkami (底筒之男大神), Okinagaranushihime no Ōkami (息長足姫大神), Onamochi no Ōkami (大名持大神), [[Sukunabikona]] no Ōkami (少彦名大神) and others are enshrined here. ==Overview== {{Citation needed|date=March 2024}} '''Sumiyoshi Shrine''' (住吉神社, Sumiyoshi Jinja) is a Shinto shrine located in [[Otaru]], [[Hokkaido]], dedicated to the Sumiyoshi deities (Sokotutsuo no Kami, Nakatutsuo no Kami, Uwatsutsuo no Kami, and Okitsuhime no Mikoto). It is currently classified as a Beppyo Shrine under the [[Association of Shinto Shrines]], and its former rank under the modern shrine ranking system was Prefectural Shrine. == History == * [[1865]] (Keio 1): Shigeakira Kikuchi, a priest from Hakodate Hachiman Shrine, petitioned the Hakodate Bugyōsho to enshrine the Sumiyoshi deities as the general guardians for the Otarunai and Takashima areas. Permission was granted to enshrine them in Otarunai. * [[1868]] (Meiji 1): The sacred objects arrived in Otarunai. Due to the unprepared shrine grounds, they were temporarily enshrined at Itsukushima Shrine, where the consecration ceremony took place. * [[1875]] (Meiji 8): The shrine was classified as a Gōsha (village shrine). * [[1881]] (Meiji 14): The shrine buildings were constructed at the current location, and the shrine was relocated there. * [[1896]] (Meiji 29): The shrine was renamed from "Suminoe Shrine" to "Sumiyoshi Shrine". * [[1906]] (Meiji 39): The shrine was elevated to Prefectural Shrine status. * [[1955]] (Showa 30): The shrine was added to the list of Beppyo Shrines under the Association of Shinto Shrines. * [[1971]] (Showa 46): To commemorate the 100th anniversary of its establishment, the shrine buildings were renovated. * [[1997]] (Heisei 9): To mark the 130th anniversary, the shrine’s mikoshi (portable shrine), the largest in Hokkaido, was restored. * [[2007]] (Heisei 19): To celebrate the 140th anniversary, a mikoshi warehouse was built to house five mikoshi and one horse-drawn carriage. == annual Grand festival == The Sumiyoshi Shrine annual Grand festival, commonly known as the "Otaru festival," is considered one of the three major festivals in Otaru.{{Cite web | url = http://www.hokkaido-np.co.jp/news/chiiki/551341.html | title = 小樽・住吉神社例大祭 露店150店連なる 16日まで | work = [[Hokkaido Shimbun]] | publisher = Hokkaido Shimbunsha | date = 2014-07-15 | access-date = 2014-07-16 | archive-url = https://web.archive.org/web/20140726221521/https://www.hokkaido-np.co.jp/news/chiiki/551341.html | archive-date = 2014-07-26 }} == Access == * [[JR Hokkaido]] [[Hakodate Main Line]] [[Minami-Otaru Station]], an 8-minute walk * [[Hokkaido Chuo Bus]] (Otaru Depot, Shin Sapporo Depot, Satsuru Line), [[JR Hokkaido Bus]] (Satsuru Line), "Sumiyoshi Shrine Mae" bus stop, a 1-minute walk == Gallery == Sumiyoshi Shrine of Otaru - Shamusho.jpg|Office (Otaru City Designated Historical Building) Sumiyoshi Shrine of Otaru - Haiden.jpg|Worship Hall Kataro Matsukawa 20210919.jpg|Statue of Katataro Matsukawa Monument to National Foundation Day at Sumiyoshi Shrine of Otaru.jpg|Monument to National Foundation Day Monument to Kitchen Knives at Sumiyoshi Shrine of Otaru.jpg|Kitchen Knife Monument Height Mesurement Stone at Sumiyoshi Shrine of Otaru.jpg|Height Measurement Stone and Child Blessing Dog File:第二次世界大戦前の住吉神社（北海道小樽市）.jpg|Sumiyoshi Shrine approach in the Taisho era (postcard dated July 9, 1919) == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat}} {{Osm box|w|665046858}} * {{Official website|url=http://www.otarusumiyoshijinja.or.jp/|name=Otaru General Guardian "Sumiyoshi Shrine"}} - Official website * [https://hokkaidojinjacho.jp/%e4%bd%8f%e5%90%89%e7%a5%9e%e7%a4%be-3/ Sumiyoshi Shrine] - Hokkaido Shrine Association {{-}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Sumiyoshi Shrine Otaru}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:Shinto shrines in Hokkaido]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Sumiyoshi Shrines]] {{Short description|Shrine in Hokkaido, Japan}} {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Sumiyoshi Shrine | image = [[File:Sumiyoshi Shrine of Otaru.jpg|250px|Sumiyoshi Shrine]]
[[Torii]] | location = [[Hokkaido]][[Otaru]] Suminoye 2-5-1 | coordinates = {{coord|43|10|58.6|N|141|0|11.6|E|region:JP-01_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{Plainlist| * [[Sokotutsuo no Mikoto|Sokotutsuo no Kami]] * [[Nakatutsuo no Mikoto|Nakatutsuo no Kami]] * [[Uwatsutsuo no Mikoto|Uwatsutsuo no Kami]] * [[Empress Jingū|Okitsuhime no Mikoto]] }} | type = {{Plainlist| * Former Prefectural Shrine * Beppyo Shrine }} | founded = [[1868]] (Meiji 1) | main hall= [[Sumiyoshi-zukuri]] | festival = July 15 | Rites = {{Plainlist| * Taishakagura Dedication festival * annual Grand festival * Autumn Grand festival }} | map = {{Switcher | {{maplink2|zoom=14|frame=yes|plain=yes|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|type=shape|stroke-width=2}} | location within Otaru | {{location map|Japan Hokkaido|lat_deg=43|lat_min=10|lat_sec=58.6|lon_deg=141|lon_min=0|lon_sec=11.6|label=Sumiyoshi Shrine|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|Alternativemap=Hokkaidō géolocalisation 000.svg}} | location within Hokkaido }} }} '''Sumiyoshi Shrine''' (住吉神社, ''Sumiyoshi Jinja'') is a [[Shinto]] shrine located in [[Otaru, Hokkaido]]. Its festival is on July 15.https://unga-plus.com/?pid=172674544 The [[kami]] Kamitsusu no O no Ōkami (上筒之男大神), Nakatsutsu no O no Ōkami (中筒之男大神), Sokotsutsu no O no Ōkami (底筒之男大神), Okinagaranushihime no Ōkami (息長足姫大神), Onamochi no Ōkami (大名持大神), [[Sukunabikona]] no Ōkami (少彦名大神) and others are enshrined here. ==Overview== {{Citation needed|date=March 2024}} '''Sumiyoshi Shrine''' (住吉神社, Sumiyoshi Jinja) is a Shinto shrine located in [[Otaru]], [[Hokkaido]], dedicated to the Sumiyoshi deities (Sokotutsuo no Kami, Nakatutsuo no Kami, Uwatsutsuo no Kami, and Okitsuhime no Mikoto). It is currently classified as a Beppyo Shrine under the [[Association of Shinto Shrines]], and its former rank under the modern shrine ranking system was Prefectural Shrine. == History == * [[1865]] (Keio 1): Shigeakira Kikuchi, a priest from Hakodate Hachiman Shrine, petitioned the Hakodate Bugyōsho to enshrine the Sumiyoshi deities as the general guardians for the Otarunai and Takashima areas. Permission was granted to enshrine them in Otarunai. * [[1868]] (Meiji 1): The sacred objects arrived in Otarunai. Due to the unprepared shrine grounds, they were temporarily enshrined at Itsukushima Shrine, where the consecration ceremony took place. * [[1875]] (Meiji 8): The shrine was classified as a Gōsha (village shrine). * [[1881]] (Meiji 14): The shrine buildings were constructed at the current location, and the shrine was relocated there. * [[1896]] (Meiji 29): The shrine was renamed from "Suminoe Shrine" to "Sumiyoshi Shrine". * [[1906]] (Meiji 39): The shrine was elevated to Prefectural Shrine status. * [[1955]] (Showa 30): The shrine was added to the list of Beppyo Shrines under the Association of Shinto Shrines. * [[1971]] (Showa 46): To commemorate the 100th anniversary of its establishment, the shrine buildings were renovated. * [[1997]] (Heisei 9): To mark the 130th anniversary, the shrine’s mikoshi (portable shrine), the largest in Hokkaido, was restored. * [[2007]] (Heisei 19): To celebrate the 140th anniversary, a mikoshi warehouse was built to house five mikoshi and one horse-drawn carriage. == annual Grand festival == The Sumiyoshi Shrine annual Grand festival, commonly known as the "Otaru festival," is considered one of the three major festivals in Otaru.{{Cite web | url = http://www.hokkaido-np.co.jp/news/chiiki/551341.html | title = 小樽・住吉神社例大祭 露店150店連なる 16日まで | work = [[Hokkaido Shimbun]] | publisher = Hokkaido Shimbunsha | date = 2014-07-15 | access-date = 2014-07-16 | archive-url = https://web.archive.org/web/20140726221521/https://www.hokkaido-np.co.jp/news/chiiki/551341.html | archive-date = 2014-07-26 }} == Access == * [[JR Hokkaido]] [[Hakodate Main Line]] [[Minami-Otaru Station]], an 8-minute walk * [[Hokkaido Chuo Bus]] (Otaru Depot, Shin Sapporo Depot, Satsuru Line), [[JR Hokkaido Bus]] (Satsuru Line), "Sumiyoshi Shrine Mae" bus stop, a 1-minute walk == Gallery == Sumiyoshi Shrine of Otaru - [[Torii]] | location = [[Hokkaido]][[Otaru]] Suminoye 2-5-1 | coordinates = {{coord|43|10|58.6|N|141|0|11.6|E|region:JP-01_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{Plainlist| * [[Sokotutsuo no Mikoto|Sokotutsuo no Kami]] * [[Nakatutsuo no Mikoto|Nakatutsuo no Kami]] * [[Uwatsutsuo no Mikoto|Uwatsutsuo no Kami]] * [[Empress Jingū|Okitsuhime no Mikoto]] }} | type = {{Plainlist| * Former Prefectural Shrine * Beppyo Shrine }} | founded = [[1868]] (Meiji 1) | main hall= [[Sumiyoshi-zukuri]] | festival = July 15 | Rites = {{Plainlist| * Taishakagura Dedication festival * annual Grand festival * Autumn Grand festival }} | map = {{Switcher | {{maplink2|zoom=14|frame=yes|plain=yes|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|type=shape|stroke-width=2}} | location within Otaru | {{location map|Japan Hokkaido|lat_deg=43|lat_min=10|lat_sec=58.6|lon_deg=141|lon_min=0|lon_sec=11.6|label=Sumiyoshi Shrine|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|Alternativemap=Hokkaidō géolocalisation 000.svg}} | location within Hokkaido }} }} '''Sumiyoshi Shrine''' (住吉神社, ''Sumiyoshi Jinja'') is a [[Shinto]] shrine located in [[Otaru, Hokkaido]]. Its festival is on July 15.https://unga-plus.com/?pid=172674544 The [[kami]] Kamitsusu no O no Ōkami (上筒之男大神), Nakatsutsu no O no Ōkami (中筒之男大神), Sokotsutsu no O no Ōkami (底筒之男大神), Okinagaranushihime no Ōkami (息長足姫大神), Onamochi no Ōkami (大名持大神), [[Sukunabikona]] no Ōkami (少彦名大神) and others are enshrined here. ==Overview== {{Citation needed|date=March 2024}} '''Sumiyoshi Shrine''' (住吉神社, Sumiyoshi Jinja) is a Shinto shrine located in [[Otaru]], [[Hokkaido]], dedicated to the Sumiyoshi deities (Sokotutsuo no Kami, Nakatutsuo no Kami, Uwatsutsuo no Kami, and Okitsuhime no Mikoto). It is currently classified as a Beppyo Shrine under the [[Association of Shinto Shrines]], and its former rank under the modern shrine ranking system was Prefectural Shrine. == History == * [[1865]] (Keio 1): Shigeakira Kikuchi, a priest from Hakodate Hachiman Shrine, petitioned the Hakodate Bugyōsho to enshrine the Sumiyoshi deities as the general guardians for the Otarunai and Takashima areas. Permission was granted to enshrine them in Otarunai. * [[1868]] (Meiji 1): The sacred objects arrived in Otarunai. Due to the unprepared shrine grounds, they were temporarily enshrined at Itsukushima Shrine, where the consecration ceremony took place. * [[1875]] (Meiji 8): The shrine was classified as a Gōsha (village shrine). * [[1881]] (Meiji 14): The shrine buildings were constructed at the current location, and the shrine was relocated there. * [[1896]] (Meiji 29): The shrine was renamed from "Suminoe Shrine" to "Sumiyoshi Shrine". * [[1906]] (Meiji 39): The shrine was elevated to Prefectural Shrine status. * [[1955]] (Showa 30): The shrine was added to the list of Beppyo Shrines under the Association of Shinto Shrines. * [[1971]] (Showa 46): To commemorate the 100th anniversary of its establishment, the shrine buildings were renovated. * [[1997]] (Heisei 9): To mark the 130th anniversary, the shrine’s mikoshi (portable shrine), the largest in Hokkaido, was restored. * [[2007]] (Heisei 19): To celebrate the 140th anniversary, a mikoshi warehouse was built to house five mikoshi and one horse-drawn carriage. == annual Grand festival == The Sumiyoshi Shrine annual Grand festival, commonly known as the "Otaru festival," is considered one of the three major festivals in Otaru.{{Cite web | url = http://www.hokkaido-np.co.jp/news/chiiki/551341.html | title = 小樽・住吉神社例大祭 露店150店連なる 16日まで | work = [[Hokkaido Shimbun]] | publisher = Hokkaido Shimbunsha | date = 2014-07-15 | access-date = 2014-07-16 | archive-url = https://web.archive.org/web/20140726221521/https://www.hokkaido-np.co.jp/news/chiiki/551341.html | archive-date = 2014-07-26 }} == Access == * [[JR Hokkaido]] [[Hakodate Main Line]] [[Minami-Otaru Station]], an 8-minute walk * [[Hokkaido Chuo Bus]] (Otaru Depot, Shin Sapporo Depot, Satsuru Line), [[JR Hokkaido Bus]] (Satsuru Line), "Sumiyoshi Shrine Mae" bus stop, a 1-minute walk == Gallery == Sumiyoshi Shrine of Otaru - Shamusho.jpg|Office (Otaru City Designated Historical Building) Sumiyoshi Shrine of Otaru - Haiden.jpg|Worship Hall Kataro Matsukawa 20210919.jpg|Statue of Katataro Matsukawa Monument to National Foundation Day at Sumiyoshi Shrine of Otaru.jpg|Monument to National Foundation Day Monument to Kitchen Knives at Sumiyoshi Shrine of Otaru.jpg|Kitchen Knife Monument Height Mesurement Stone at Sumiyoshi Shrine of Otaru.jpg|Height Measurement Stone and Child Blessing Dog File:第二次世界大戦前の住吉神社（北海道小樽市）.jpg|Sumiyoshi Shrine approach in the Taisho era (postcard dated July 9, 1919) == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat}} {{Osm box|w|665046858}} * {{Official website|url=http://www.otarusumiyoshijinja.or.jp/|name=Otaru General Guardian "Sumiyoshi Shrine"}} - Due to the unprepared shrine grounds, they were temporarily enshrined at Itsukushima Shrine, where the consecration ceremony took place. * [[1875]] (Meiji 8): The shrine was classified as a Gōsha (village shrine). * [[1881]] (Meiji 14): The shrine buildings were constructed at the current location, and the shrine was relocated there. * [[1896]] (Meiji 29): The shrine was renamed from "Suminoe Shrine" to "Sumiyoshi Shrine". * [[1906]] (Meiji 39): The shrine was elevated to Prefectural Shrine status. * [[1955]] (Showa 30): The shrine was added to the list of Beppyo Shrines under the Association of Shinto Shrines. * [[1971]] (Showa 46): To commemorate the 100th anniversary of its establishment, the shrine buildings were renovated. * [[1997]] (Heisei 9): To mark the 130th anniversary, the shrine’s mikoshi (portable shrine), the largest in Hokkaido, was restored. * [[2007]] (Heisei 19): To celebrate the 140th anniversary, a mikoshi warehouse was built to house five mikoshi and one horse-drawn carriage. == annual Grand festival == The Sumiyoshi Shrine annual Grand festival, commonly known as the "Otaru festival," is considered one of the three major festivals in Otaru.{{Cite web | url = http://www.hokkaido-np.co.jp/news/chiiki/551341.html | title = 小樽・住吉神社例大祭 露店150店連なる 16日まで | work = [[Hokkaido Shimbun]] | publisher = Hokkaido Shimbunsha | date = 2014-07-15 | access-date = 2014-07-16 | archive-url = https://web.archive.org/web/20140726221521/https://www.hokkaido-np.co.jp/news/chiiki/551341.html | archive-date = 2014-07-26 }} == Access == * [[JR Hokkaido]] [[Hakodate Main Line]] [[Minami-Otaru Station]], an 8-minute walk * [[Hokkaido Chuo Bus]] (Otaru Depot, Shin Sapporo Depot, Satsuru Line), [[JR Hokkaido Bus]] (Satsuru Line), "Sumiyoshi Shrine Mae" bus stop, a 1-minute walk == Gallery == Sumiyoshi Shrine of Otaru - {{Short description|Shrine in Hokkaido, Japan}} {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Sumiyoshi Shrine | image = [[File:Sumiyoshi Shrine of Otaru.jpg|250px|Sumiyoshi Shrine]]
[[Torii]] | location = [[Hokkaido]][[Otaru]] Suminoye 2-5-1 | coordinates = {{coord|43|10|58.6|N|141|0|11.6|E|region:JP-01_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{Plainlist| * [[Sokotutsuo no Mikoto|Sokotutsuo no Kami]] * [[Nakatutsuo no Mikoto|Nakatutsuo no Kami]] * [[Uwatsutsuo no Mikoto|Uwatsutsuo no Kami]] * [[Empress Jingū|Okitsuhime no Mikoto]] }} | type = {{Plainlist| * Former Prefectural Shrine * Beppyo Shrine }} | founded = [[1868]] (Meiji 1) | main hall= [[Sumiyoshi-zukuri]] | festival = July 15 | Rites = {{Plainlist| * Taishakagura Dedication festival * annual Grand festival * Autumn Grand festival }} | map = {{Switcher | {{maplink2|zoom=14|frame=yes|plain=yes|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|type=shape|stroke-width=2}} | location within Otaru | {{location map|Japan Hokkaido|lat_deg=43|lat_min=10|lat_sec=58.6|lon_deg=141|lon_min=0|lon_sec=11.6|label=Sumiyoshi Shrine|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|Alternativemap=Hokkaidō géolocalisation 000.svg}} | location within Hokkaido }} }} '''Sumiyoshi Shrine''' (住吉神社, ''Sumiyoshi Jinja'') is a [[Shinto]] shrine located in [[Otaru, Hokkaido]]. Its festival is on July 15.https://unga-plus.com/?pid=172674544 The [[kami]] Kamitsusu no O no Ōkami (上筒之男大神), Nakatsutsu no O no Ōkami (中筒之男大神), Sokotsutsu no O no Ōkami (底筒之男大神), Okinagaranushihime no Ōkami (息長足姫大神), Onamochi no Ōkami (大名持大神), [[Sukunabikona]] no Ōkami (少彦名大神) and others are enshrined here. ==Overview== {{Citation needed|date=March 2024}} '''Sumiyoshi Shrine''' (住吉神社, Sumiyoshi Jinja) is a Shinto shrine located in [[Otaru]], [[Hokkaido]], dedicated to the Sumiyoshi deities (Sokotutsuo no Kami, Nakatutsuo no Kami, Uwatsutsuo no Kami, and Okitsuhime no Mikoto). It is currently classified as a Beppyo Shrine under the [[Association of Shinto Shrines]], and its former rank under the modern shrine ranking system was Prefectural Shrine. == History == * [[1865]] (Keio 1): Shigeakira Kikuchi, a priest from Hakodate Hachiman Shrine, petitioned the Hakodate Bugyōsho to enshrine the Sumiyoshi deities as the general guardians for the Otarunai and Takashima areas. Permission was granted to enshrine them in Otarunai. * [[1868]] (Meiji 1): The sacred objects arrived in Otarunai. Due to the unprepared shrine grounds, they were temporarily enshrined at Itsukushima Shrine, where the consecration ceremony took place. * [[1875]] (Meiji 8): The shrine was classified as a Gōsha (village shrine). * [[1881]] (Meiji 14): The shrine buildings were constructed at the current location, and the shrine was relocated there. * [[1896]] (Meiji 29): The shrine was renamed from "Suminoe Shrine" to "Sumiyoshi Shrine". * [[1906]] (Meiji 39): The shrine was elevated to Prefectural Shrine status. * [[1955]] (Showa 30): The shrine was added to the list of Beppyo Shrines under the Association of Shinto Shrines. * [[1971]] (Showa 46): To commemorate the 100th anniversary of its establishment, the shrine buildings were renovated. * [[1997]] (Heisei 9): To mark the 130th anniversary, the shrine’s mikoshi (portable shrine), the largest in Hokkaido, was restored. * [[2007]] (Heisei 19): To celebrate the 140th anniversary, a mikoshi warehouse was built to house five mikoshi and one horse-drawn carriage. == annual Grand festival == The Sumiyoshi Shrine annual Grand festival, commonly known as the "Otaru festival," is considered one of the three major festivals in Otaru.{{Cite web | url = http://www.hokkaido-np.co.jp/news/chiiki/551341.html | title = 小樽・住吉神社例大祭 露店150店連なる 16日まで | work = [[Hokkaido Shimbun]] | publisher = Hokkaido Shimbunsha | date = 2014-07-15 | access-date = 2014-07-16 | archive-url = https://web.archive.org/web/20140726221521/https://www.hokkaido-np.co.jp/news/chiiki/551341.html | archive-date = 2014-07-26 }} == Access == * [[JR Hokkaido]] [[Hakodate Main Line]] [[Minami-Otaru Station]], an 8-minute walk * [[Hokkaido Chuo Bus]] (Otaru Depot, Shin Sapporo Depot, Satsuru Line), [[JR Hokkaido Bus]] (Satsuru Line), "Sumiyoshi Shrine Mae" bus stop, a 1-minute walk == Gallery == Sumiyoshi Shrine of Otaru - Shamusho.jpg|Office (Otaru City Designated Historical Building) Sumiyoshi Shrine of Otaru - Haiden.jpg|Worship Hall Kataro Matsukawa 20210919.jpg|Statue of Katataro Matsukawa Monument to National Foundation Day at Sumiyoshi Shrine of Otaru.jpg|Monument to National Foundation Day Monument to Kitchen Knives at Sumiyoshi Shrine of Otaru.jpg|Kitchen Knife Monument Height Mesurement Stone at Sumiyoshi Shrine of Otaru.jpg|Height Measurement Stone and Child Blessing Dog File:第二次世界大戦前の住吉神社（北海道小樽市）.jpg|Sumiyoshi Shrine approach in the Taisho era (postcard dated July 9, 1919) == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat}} {{Osm box|w|665046858}} * {{Official website|url=http://www.otarusumiyoshijinja.or.jp/|name=Otaru General Guardian "Sumiyoshi Shrine"}} - {{Short description|Shrine in Hokkaido, Japan}} {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Sumiyoshi Shrine | image = [[File:Sumiyoshi Shrine of Otaru.jpg|250px|Sumiyoshi Shrine]]
[[Torii]] | location = [[Hokkaido]][[Otaru]] Suminoye 2-5-1 | coordinates = {{coord|43|10|58.6|N|141|0|11.6|E|region:JP-01_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{Plainlist| * [[Sokotutsuo no Mikoto|Sokotutsuo no Kami]] * [[Nakatutsuo no Mikoto|Nakatutsuo no Kami]] * [[Uwatsutsuo no Mikoto|Uwatsutsuo no Kami]] * [[Empress Jingū|Okitsuhime no Mikoto]] }} | type = {{Plainlist| * Former Prefectural Shrine * Beppyo Shrine }} | founded = [[1868]] (Meiji 1) | main hall= [[Sumiyoshi-zukuri]] | festival = July 15 | Rites = {{Plainlist| * Taishakagura Dedication festival * annual Grand festival * Autumn Grand festival }} | map = {{Switcher | {{maplink2|zoom=14|frame=yes|plain=yes|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|type=shape|stroke-width=2}} | location within Otaru | {{location map|Japan Hokkaido|lat_deg=43|lat_min=10|lat_sec=58.6|lon_deg=141|lon_min=0|lon_sec=11.6|label=Sumiyoshi Shrine|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|Alternativemap=Hokkaidō géolocalisation 000.svg}} | location within Hokkaido }} }} '''Sumiyoshi Shrine''' (住吉神社, ''Sumiyoshi Jinja'') is a [[Shinto]] shrine located in [[Otaru, Hokkaido]]. Its festival is on July 15.https://unga-plus.com/?pid=172674544 The [[kami]] Kamitsusu no O no Ōkami (上筒之男大神), Nakatsutsu no O no Ōkami (中筒之男大神), Sokotsutsu no O no Ōkami (底筒之男大神), Okinagaranushihime no Ōkami (息長足姫大神), Onamochi no Ōkami (大名持大神), [[Sukunabikona]] no Ōkami (少彦名大神) and others are enshrined here. ==Overview== {{Citation needed|date=March 2024}} '''Sumiyoshi Shrine''' (住吉神社, Sumiyoshi Jinja) is a Shinto shrine located in [[Otaru]], [[Hokkaido]], dedicated to the Sumiyoshi deities (Sokotutsuo no Kami, Nakatutsuo no Kami, Uwatsutsuo no Kami, and Okitsuhime no Mikoto). It is currently classified as a Beppyo Shrine under the [[Association of Shinto Shrines]], and its former rank under the modern shrine ranking system was Prefectural Shrine. == History == * [[1865]] (Keio 1): Shigeakira Kikuchi, a priest from Hakodate Hachiman Shrine, petitioned the Hakodate Bugyōsho to enshrine the Sumiyoshi deities as the general guardians for the Otarunai and Takashima areas. Permission was granted to enshrine them in Otarunai. * [[1868]] (Meiji 1): The sacred objects arrived in Otarunai. Due to the unprepared shrine grounds, they were temporarily enshrined at Itsukushima Shrine, where the consecration ceremony took place. * [[1875]] (Meiji 8): The shrine was classified as a Gōsha (village shrine). * [[1881]] (Meiji 14): The shrine buildings were constructed at the current location, and the shrine was relocated there. * [[1896]] (Meiji 29): The shrine was renamed from "Suminoe Shrine" to "Sumiyoshi Shrine". * [[1906]] (Meiji 39): The shrine was elevated to Prefectural Shrine status. * [[1955]] (Showa 30): The shrine was added to the list of Beppyo Shrines under the Association of Shinto Shrines. * [[1971]] (Showa 46): To commemorate the 100th anniversary of its establishment, the shrine buildings were renovated. * [[1997]] (Heisei 9): To mark the 130th anniversary, the shrine’s mikoshi (portable shrine), the largest in Hokkaido, was restored. * [[2007]] (Heisei 19): To celebrate the 140th anniversary, a mikoshi warehouse was built to house five mikoshi and one horse-drawn carriage. == annual Grand festival == The Sumiyoshi Shrine annual Grand festival, commonly known as the "Otaru festival," is considered one of the three major festivals in Otaru.{{Cite web | url = http://www.hokkaido-np.co.jp/news/chiiki/551341.html | title = 小樽・住吉神社例大祭 露店150店連なる 16日まで | work = [[Hokkaido Shimbun]] | publisher = Hokkaido Shimbunsha | date = 2014-07-15 | access-date = 2014-07-16 | archive-url = https://web.archive.org/web/20140726221521/https://www.hokkaido-np.co.jp/news/chiiki/551341.html | archive-date = 2014-07-26 }} == Access == * [[JR Hokkaido]] [[Hakodate Main Line]] [[Minami-Otaru Station]], an 8-minute walk * [[Hokkaido Chuo Bus]] (Otaru Depot, Shin Sapporo Depot, Satsuru Line), [[JR Hokkaido Bus]] (Satsuru Line), "Sumiyoshi Shrine Mae" bus stop, a 1-minute walk == Gallery == Sumiyoshi Shrine of Otaru - Shamusho.jpg|Office (Otaru City Designated Historical Building) Sumiyoshi Shrine of Otaru - Haiden.jpg|Worship Hall Kataro Matsukawa 20210919.jpg|Statue of Katataro Matsukawa Monument to National Foundation Day at Sumiyoshi Shrine of Otaru.jpg|Monument to National Foundation Day Monument to Kitchen Knives at Sumiyoshi Shrine of Otaru.jpg|Kitchen Knife Monument Height Mesurement Stone at Sumiyoshi Shrine of Otaru.jpg|Height Measurement Stone and Child Blessing Dog File:第二次世界大戦前の住吉神社（北海道小樽市）.jpg|Sumiyoshi Shrine approach in the Taisho era (postcard dated July 9, 1919) == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat}} {{Osm box|w|665046858}} * {{Official website|url=http://www.otarusumiyoshijinja.or.jp/|name=Otaru General Guardian "Sumiyoshi Shrine"}} - Official website * [https://hokkaidojinjacho.jp/%e4%bd%8f%e5%90%89%e7%a5%9e%e7%a4%be-3/ Sumiyoshi Shrine] - Hokkaido Shrine Association {{-}} {{shinto-stub}} ==See also== *[[List of Shinto shrines in Japan#Hokkaid.C5.8D|List of Shinto shrines in Hokkaidō]] ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External links== *[https://hokkaidojinjacho.jp/住三吉神社/ Hokkaido Shinto listing] {{Shinto shrine}} {{coord missing|Hokkaidō Prefecture}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Sumiyoshi Faith}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hokkaido]] [[Category:Sumiyoshi Shrines]] [[Category:History of Otaru]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Otaru]] [[Category:Otaru City Designated Historical Buildings]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Sumiyoshi shrines]] [[Category:Expanded pages from Wikipedia]] [[Category:Sumiyoshi Shrine (Hokkaidō)]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Sumiyoshi Shrine (Hokkaidō)]] [[en:Sumiyoshi Shrine (Hokkaidō)]] [[ja:住吉神社 (小樽市)]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Sumiyoshi Shrine Otaru}}

Its festival is on July 15.https://unga-plus.com/?pid=172674544 The [[kami]] Kamitsusu no O no Ōkami (上筒之男大神), Nakatsutsu no O no Ōkami (中筒之男大神), Sokotsutsu no O no Ōkami (底筒之男大神), Okinagaranushihime no Ōkami (息長足姫大神), Onamochi no Ōkami (大名持大神), [[Sukunabikona]] no Ōkami (少彦名大神) and others are enshrined here. ==Overview== {{Citation needed|date=March 2024}} '''Sumiyoshi Shrine''' (住吉神社, Sumiyoshi Jinja) is a Shinto shrine located in [[Otaru]], [[Hokkaido]], dedicated to the Sumiyoshi deities (Sokotutsuo no Kami, Nakatutsuo no Kami, Uwatsutsuo no Kami, and Okitsuhime no Mikoto). It is currently classified as a Beppyo Shrine under the [[Association of Shinto Shrines]], and its former rank under the modern shrine ranking system was Prefectural Shrine. == History == * [[1865]] (Keio 1): Shigeakira Kikuchi, a priest from Hakodate Hachiman Shrine, petitioned the Hakodate Bugyōsho to enshrine the Sumiyoshi deities as the general guardians for the Otarunai and Takashima areas. Permission was granted to enshrine them in Otarunai. * [[1868]] (Meiji 1): The sacred objects arrived in Otarunai. Due to the unprepared shrine grounds, they were temporarily enshrined at Itsukushima Shrine, where the consecration ceremony took place. * [[1875]] (Meiji 8): The shrine was classified as a Gōsha (village shrine). * [[1881]] (Meiji 14): The shrine buildings were constructed at the current location, and the shrine was relocated there. * [[1896]] (Meiji 29): The shrine was renamed from "Suminoe Shrine" to "Sumiyoshi Shrine". * [[1906]] (Meiji 39): The shrine was elevated to Prefectural Shrine status. * [[1955]] (Showa 30): The shrine was added to the list of Beppyo Shrines under the Association of Shinto Shrines. * [[1971]] (Showa 46): To commemorate the 100th anniversary of its establishment, the shrine buildings were renovated. * [[1997]] (Heisei 9): To mark the 130th anniversary, the shrine’s mikoshi (portable shrine), the largest in Hokkaido, was restored. * [[2007]] (Heisei 19): To celebrate the 140th anniversary, a mikoshi warehouse was built to house five mikoshi and one horse-drawn carriage. == annual Grand festival == The Sumiyoshi Shrine annual Grand festival, commonly known as the "Otaru festival," is considered one of the three major festivals in Otaru.{{Cite web | url = http://www.hokkaido-np.co.jp/news/chiiki/551341.html | title = 小樽・住吉神社例大祭 露店150店連なる 16日まで | work = [[Hokkaido Shimbun]] | publisher = Hokkaido Shimbunsha | date = 2014-07-15 | access-date = 2014-07-16 | archive-url = https://web.archive.org/web/20140726221521/https://www.hokkaido-np.co.jp/news/chiiki/551341.html | archive-date = 2014-07-26 }} == Access == * [[JR Hokkaido]] [[Hakodate Main Line]] [[Minami-Otaru Station]], an 8-minute walk * [[Hokkaido Chuo Bus]] (Otaru Depot, Shin Sapporo Depot, Satsuru Line), [[JR Hokkaido Bus]] (Satsuru Line), "Sumiyoshi Shrine Mae" bus stop, a 1-minute walk == Gallery == Sumiyoshi Shrine of Otaru - Shamusho.jpg|Office (Otaru City Designated Historical Building) Sumiyoshi Shrine of Otaru - Haiden.jpg|Worship Hall Kataro Matsukawa 20210919.jpg|Statue of Katataro Matsukawa Monument to National Foundation Day at Sumiyoshi Shrine of Otaru.jpg|Monument to National Foundation Day Monument to Kitchen Knives at Sumiyoshi Shrine of Otaru.jpg|Kitchen Knife Monument Height Mesurement Stone at Sumiyoshi Shrine of Otaru.jpg|Height Measurement Stone and Child Blessing Dog File:第二次世界大戦前の住吉神社（北海道小樽市）.jpg|Sumiyoshi Shrine approach in the Taisho era (postcard dated July 9, 1919) == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat}} {{Osm box|w|665046858}} * {{Official website|url=http://www.otarusumiyoshijinja.or.jp/|name=Otaru General Guardian "Sumiyoshi Shrine"}} - It is currently classified as a Beppyo Shrine under the [[Association of Shinto Shrines]], and its former Rank under the modern shrine Ranking system was Prefectural Shrine. == History == * [[1865]] (Keio 1): Shigeakira Kikuchi, a priest from Hakodate Hachiman Shrine, petitioned the Hakodate Bugyōsho to enshrine the Sumiyoshi deities as the general guardians for the Otarunai and Takashima areas. Permission was granted to enshrine them in Otarunai. * [[1868]] (Meiji 1): The sacred objects arrived in Otarunai. Due to the unprepared shrine grounds, they were temporarily enshrined at Itsukushima Shrine, where the consecration ceremony took place. * [[1875]] (Meiji 8): The shrine was classified as a Gōsha (village shrine). * [[1881]] (Meiji 14): The shrine buildings were constructed at the current location, and the shrine was relocated there. * [[1896]] (Meiji 29): The shrine was renamed from "Suminoe Shrine" to "Sumiyoshi Shrine". * [[1906]] (Meiji 39): The shrine was elevated to Prefectural Shrine status. * [[1955]] (Showa 30): The shrine was added to the list of Beppyo Shrines under the Association of Shinto Shrines. * [[1971]] (Showa 46): To commemorate the 100th anniversary of its establishment, the shrine buildings were renovated. * [[1997]] (Heisei 9): To mark the 130th anniversary, the shrine’s mikoshi (portable shrine), the largest in Hokkaido, was restored. * [[2007]] (Heisei 19): To celebrate the 140th anniversary, a mikoshi warehouse was built to house five mikoshi and one horse-drawn carriage. == annual Grand festival == The Sumiyoshi Shrine annual Grand festival, commonly known as the "Otaru festival," is considered one of the three major festivals in Otaru.{{Cite web | url = http://www.hokkaido-np.co.jp/news/chiiki/551341.html | title = 小樽・住吉神社例大祭 露店150店連なる 16日まで | work = [[Hokkaido Shimbun]] | publisher = Hokkaido Shimbunsha | date = 2014-07-15 | access-date = 2014-07-16 | archive-url = https://web.archive.org/web/20140726221521/https://www.hokkaido-np.co.jp/news/chiiki/551341.html | archive-date = 2014-07-26 }} == Access == * [[JR Hokkaido]] [[Hakodate Main Line]] [[Minami-Otaru Station]], an 8-minute walk * [[Hokkaido Chuo Bus]] (Otaru Depot, Shin Sapporo Depot, Satsuru Line), [[JR Hokkaido Bus]] (Satsuru Line), "Sumiyoshi Shrine Mae" bus stop, a 1-minute walk == Gallery == Sumiyoshi Shrine of Otaru - Shamusho.jpg|Office (Otaru City Designated Historical Building) Sumiyoshi Shrine of Otaru - Haiden.jpg|Worship Hall Kataro Matsukawa 20210919.jpg|Statue of Katataro Matsukawa Monument to National Foundation Day at Sumiyoshi Shrine of Otaru.jpg|Monument to National Foundation Day Monument to Kitchen Knives at Sumiyoshi Shrine of Otaru.jpg|Kitchen Knife Monument Height Mesurement Stone at Sumiyoshi Shrine of Otaru.jpg|Height Measurement Stone and Child Blessing Dog File:第二次世界大戦前の住吉神社（北海道小樽市）.jpg|Sumiyoshi Shrine approach in the Taisho era (postcard dated July 9, 1919) == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat}} {{Osm box|w|665046858}} * {{Official website|url=http://www.otarusumiyoshijinja.or.jp/|name=Otaru General Guardian "Sumiyoshi Shrine"}} - Official website * [https://hokkaidojinjacho.jp/%e4%bd%8f%e5%90%89%e7%a5%9e%e7%a4%be-3/ Sumiyoshi Shrine] - Hokkaido Shrine Association {{-}} {{shinto-stub}} ==See also== *[[List of Shinto shrines in Japan#Hokkaid.C5.8D|List of Shinto shrines in Hokkaidō]] ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External links== *[https://hokkaidojinjacho.jp/住三吉神社/ Hokkaido Shinto listing] {{Shinto shrine}} {{coord missing|Hokkaidō Prefecture}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Sumiyoshi Faith}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hokkaido]] [[Category:Sumiyoshi Shrines]] [[Category:History of Otaru]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Otaru]] [[Category:Otaru City Designated Historical Buildings]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Sumiyoshi shrines]] [[Category:Expanded pages from Wikipedia]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Sumiyoshi Shrine (Hokkaidō)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Sumiyoshi Shrine (Hokkaidō)]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Sumiyoshi Shrine (Hokkaidō)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Sumiyoshi Shrine (Hokkaidō)]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Sumiyoshi Shrine (Hokkaidō)]] [[en:Sumiyoshi Shrine (Hokkaidō)]] [[ja:住吉神社 (小樽市)]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Sumiyoshi Shrine Otaru}}

{{出典の明記|date=2024年3月}} {{神社 | 名称 = 住吉神社 | 画像 = [[File:Sumiyoshi Shrine of Otaru.jpg|250px|住吉神社]]
[[鳥居]] | 所在地 = [[北海道]][[小樽市]]住ノ江2-5-1 | 位置 = {{coord|43|10|58.6|N|141|0|11.6|E|region:JP-01_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | 祭神 = {{Plainlist| * [[底筒男神]] * [[中筒男神]] * [[表筒男神]] * [[息長帯姫命]] }} | 社格 = {{Plainlist| * 旧[[県社]] * [[別表神社]] }} | 創建 = [[1868年]]（[[明治]]元年） | 本殿 = [[住吉造]] | 例祭 = [[7月15日]] | 神事 = {{Plainlist| * 太々神楽奉納祭 * 例大祭 * 秋季大祭 }} | 地図 = {{Switcher | {{maplink2|zoom=14|frame=yes|plain=yes|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|type=shape|stroke-width=2}} | 小樽市内の位置 | {{Location map|Japan Hokkaido|lat_deg=43|lat_min=10|lat_sec=58.6|lon_deg=141|lon_min=0|lon_sec=11.6|label=住吉神社|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|AlternativeMap=Hokkaidō géolocalisation 000.svg}} | 北海道内の位置 }} }} '''住吉神社'''（すみよしじんじゃ）は、[[北海道]][[小樽市]]に鎮座する[[住吉三神#住吉三神を祀る主な神社|住吉神社]]。住吉神（[[住吉三神|底筒男神・中筒男神・表筒男神]]・[[神功皇后|息長帯姫命]]）を祀る。 現在は[[神社本庁]]包括下（北海道神社庁後志支部管内）の[[別表神社]]で、[[近代社格制度|旧社格]]は[[県社]]。 ==歴史== * [[1865年]]（[[慶応]]元年）：[[函館八幡宮]][[神職|神主]]の菊地重賢が、[[小樽郡|オタルナイ]]・[[高島郡 (北海道)|タカシマ]]両地区の[[総鎮守]]として住吉神の[[勧請]]を[[遠国奉行#箱館奉行・松前奉行・蝦夷奉行|箱館奉行所]]に願い出、オタルナイに勧請・創建が許可された。 * [[1868年]]（[[明治]]元年）：オタルナイに[[神体]]が到着し、社地の造成が間に合わなかったため厳島神社に仮に奉斎して鎮座祭が行われた。 * [[1875年]]（明治8年）：[[郷社]]に列格。 * [[1881年]]（明治14年）：現在地に社殿を造営して遷座。 * [[1896年]]（明治29年）：「墨江神社」から「住吉神社」へに改称。 * [[1906年]]（明治39年）：県社に昇格。 * [[1955年]]（[[昭和]]30年）：[[神社本庁]]の[[別表神社]]に加列。 * [[1971年]]（昭和46年）7月：鎮座100年を記念して社殿を改築。 * [[1997年]]（[[平成]]9年）：鎮座130年を記念して道内最大級の[[神輿]]「百貫神輿」を修復。 * [[2007年]]（平成19年）：鎮座140年を記念して神輿五基、[[馬車]]一台を奉安する神輿蔵を建設。 == 例大祭 == 住吉神社例大祭（通称「小樽まつり」）は小樽三大祭の一つに数えられている{{Cite web | url = http://www.hokkaido-np.co.jp/news/chiiki/551341.html | title = 小樽・住吉神社例大祭 露店150店連なる 16日まで | work = [[北海道新聞]] | publisher = 北海道新聞社 | date = 2014-07-15 | access-date = 2014-07-16 | archive-url = https://web.archive.org/web/20140726221521/https://www.hokkaido-np.co.jp/news/chiiki/551341.html | archive-date = 2014-07-26 }}。 == 交通 == * [[北海道旅客鉄道|JR]][[函館本線]][[南小樽駅]]下車徒歩8分 * [[北海道中央バス]]（[[北海道中央バスおたもい営業所|おたもい営業所]]、[[北海道中央バス真栄営業所|真栄営業所]]、[[札樽線 (北海道中央バス)|札樽線]]）[[ジェイ・アール北海道バス]]（[[札樽線 (ジェイ・アール北海道バス)|札樽線]]）「住吉神社前」バス停下車徒歩1分 ==ギャラリー== Sumiyoshi Shrine of Otaru - Shamusho.jpg|社務所（[[小樽市指定歴史的建造物]]） Sumiyoshi Shrine of Otaru - Haiden.jpg|拝殿 Kataro Matsukawa 20210919.jpg|松川嘉太郎翁像 Monument to National Foundation Day at Sumiyoshi Shrine of Otaru.jpg|「[[建国記念の日]]」碑 Monument to Kitchen Knives at Sumiyoshi Shrine of Otaru.jpg|包丁塚 Height Mesurement Stone at Sumiyoshi Shrine of Otaru.jpg|せたけ石・子宝いぬ 第二次世界大戦前の住吉神社（北海道小樽市）.jpg|[[大正時代]]の住吉神社参道（1919年7月9日差し出しの絵葉書） == 脚注 == *[https://hokkaidojinjacho.jp/%e4%bd%8f%e5%90%89%e7%a5%9e%e7%a4%be-3/ 住吉神社] - 北海道神社庁 {{-}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:すみよししんしや おたるし}} [[Category:北海道の神社]] [[Category:北海道の旧県社]] [[Category:別表神社]] {{Short description|Shrine in Hokkaido, Japan}} {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Sumiyoshi Shrine | image = [[File:Sumiyoshi Shrine of Otaru.jpg|250px|Sumiyoshi Shrine]]
[[Torii]] | location = [[Hokkaido]][[Otaru]] Suminoye 2-5-1 | coordinates = {{coord|43|10|58.6|N|141|0|11.6|E|region:JP-01_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{Plainlist| * [[Sokotutsuo no Mikoto|Sokotutsuo no Kami]] * [[Nakatutsuo no Mikoto|Nakatutsuo no Kami]] * [[Uwatsutsuo no Mikoto|Uwatsutsuo no Kami]] * [[Empress Jingū|Okitsuhime no Mikoto]] }} | type = {{Plainlist| * Former Prefectural Shrine * Beppyo Shrine }} | founded = [[1868]] (Meiji 1) | main hall= [[Sumiyoshi-zukuri]] | festival = July 15 | Rites = {{Plainlist| * Taishakagura Dedication festival * annual Grand festival * Autumn Grand festival }} | map = {{Switcher | {{maplink2|zoom=14|frame=yes|plain=yes|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|type=shape|stroke-width=2}} | location within Otaru | {{location map|Japan Hokkaido|lat_deg=43|lat_min=10|lat_sec=58.6|lon_deg=141|lon_min=0|lon_sec=11.6|label=Sumiyoshi Shrine|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|Alternativemap=Hokkaidō géolocalisation 000.svg}} | location within Hokkaido }} }} '''Sumiyoshi Shrine''' (住吉神社, ''Sumiyoshi Jinja'') is a [[Shinto]] shrine located in [[Otaru, Hokkaido]]. Its festival is on July 15.https://unga-plus.com/?pid=172674544 The [[kami]] Kamitsusu no O no Ōkami (上筒之男大神), Nakatsutsu no O no Ōkami (中筒之男大神), Sokotsutsu no O no Ōkami (底筒之男大神), Okinagaranushihime no Ōkami (息長足姫大神), Onamochi no Ōkami (大名持大神), [[Sukunabikona]] no Ōkami (少彦名大神) and others are enshrined here. ==Overview== {{Citation needed|date=March 2024}} '''Sumiyoshi Shrine''' (住吉神社, Sumiyoshi Jinja) is a Shinto shrine located in [[Otaru]], [[Hokkaido]], dedicated to the Sumiyoshi deities (Sokotutsuo no Kami, Nakatutsuo no Kami, Uwatsutsuo no Kami, and Okitsuhime no Mikoto). It is currently classified as a Beppyo Shrine under the [[Association of Shinto Shrines]], and its former Rank under the modern shrine Ranking system was Prefectural Shrine. == History == * [[1865]] (Keio 1): Shigeakira Kikuchi, a priest from Hakodate Hachiman Shrine, petitioned the Hakodate Bugyōsho to enshrine the Sumiyoshi deities as the general guardians for the Otarunai and Takashima areas. Permission was granted to enshrine them in Otarunai. * [[1868]] (Meiji 1): The sacred objects arrived in Otarunai. Due to the unprepared shrine grounds, they were temporarily enshrined at Itsukushima Shrine, where the consecration ceremony took place. * [[1875]] (Meiji 8): The shrine was classified as a Gōsha (village shrine). * [[1881]] (Meiji 14): The shrine buildings were constructed at the current location, and the shrine was relocated there. * [[1896]] (Meiji 29): The shrine was renamed from "Suminoe Shrine" to "Sumiyoshi Shrine". * [[1906]] (Meiji 39): The shrine was elevated to Prefectural Shrine status. * [[1955]] (Showa 30): The shrine was added to the list of Beppyo Shrines under the Association of Shinto Shrines. * [[1971]] (Showa 46): To commemorate the 100th anniversary of its establishment, the shrine buildings were renovated. * [[1997]] (Heisei 9): To mark the 130th anniversary, the shrine’s mikoshi (portable shrine), the largest in Hokkaido, was restored. * [[2007]] (Heisei 19): To celebrate the 140th anniversary, a mikoshi warehouse was built to house five mikoshi and one horse-drawn carriage. == annual Grand festival == The Sumiyoshi Shrine annual Grand festival, commonly known as the "Otaru festival," is considered one of the three major festivals in Otaru.{{Cite web | url = http://www.hokkaido-np.co.jp/news/chiiki/551341.html | title = 小樽・住吉神社例大祭 露店150店連なる 16日まで | work = [[Hokkaido Shimbun]] | publisher = Hokkaido Shimbunsha | date = 2014-07-15 | access-date = 2014-07-16 | archive-url = https://web.archive.org/web/20140726221521/https://www.hokkaido-np.co.jp/news/chiiki/551341.html | archive-date = 2014-07-26 }} == Access == * [[JR Hokkaido]] [[Hakodate Main Line]] [[Minami-Otaru Station]], an 8-minute walk * [[Hokkaido Chuo Bus]] (Otaru Depot, Shin Sapporo Depot, Satsuru Line), [[JR Hokkaido Bus]] (Satsuru Line), "Sumiyoshi Shrine Mae" bus stop, a 1-minute walk == Gallery == Sumiyoshi Shrine of Otaru - Shamusho.jpg|Office (Otaru City Designated Historical Building) Sumiyoshi Shrine of Otaru - Haiden.jpg|Worship Hall Kataro Matsukawa 20210919.jpg|Statue of Katataro Matsukawa Monument to National Foundation Day at Sumiyoshi Shrine of Otaru.jpg|Monument to National Foundation Day Monument to Kitchen Knives at Sumiyoshi Shrine of Otaru.jpg|Kitchen Knife Monument Height Mesurement Stone at Sumiyoshi Shrine of Otaru.jpg|Height Measurement Stone and Child Blessing Dog File:第二次世界大戦前の住吉神社（北海道小樽市）.jpg|Sumiyoshi Shrine approach in the Taisho era (postcard dated July 9, 1919) == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat}} {{Osm box|w|665046858}} * {{Official website|url=http://www.otarusumiyoshijinja.or.jp/|name=Otaru General Guardian "Sumiyoshi Shrine"}} - {{Short description|Shrine in Hokkaido, Japan}} {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Sumiyoshi Shrine | image = [[File:Sumiyoshi Shrine of Otaru.jpg|250px|Sumiyoshi Shrine]]
[[Torii]] | location = [[Hokkaido]][[Otaru]] Suminoye 2-5-1 | coordinates = {{coord|43|10|58.6|N|141|0|11.6|E|region:JP-01_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{Plainlist| * [[Sokotutsuo no Mikoto|Sokotutsuo no Kami]] * [[Nakatutsuo no Mikoto|Nakatutsuo no Kami]] * [[Uwatsutsuo no Mikoto|Uwatsutsuo no Kami]] * [[Empress Jingū|Okitsuhime no Mikoto]] }} | type = {{Plainlist| * Former Prefectural Shrine * Beppyo Shrine }} | founded = [[1868]] (Meiji 1) | main hall= [[Sumiyoshi-zukuri]] | festival = July 15 | Rites = {{Plainlist| * Taishakagura Dedication festival * annual Grand festival * Autumn Grand festival }} | map = {{Switcher | {{maplink2|zoom=14|frame=yes|plain=yes|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|type=shape|stroke-width=2}} | location within Otaru | {{location map|Japan Hokkaido|lat_deg=43|lat_min=10|lat_sec=58.6|lon_deg=141|lon_min=0|lon_sec=11.6|label=Sumiyoshi Shrine|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|Alternativemap=Hokkaidō géolocalisation 000.svg}} | location within Hokkaido }} }} '''Sumiyoshi Shrine''' (住吉神社, ''Sumiyoshi Jinja'') is a [[Shinto]] shrine located in [[Otaru, Hokkaido]]. Its festival is on July 15.https://unga-plus.com/?pid=172674544 The [[kami]] Kamitsusu no O no Ōkami (上筒之男大神), Nakatsutsu no O no Ōkami (中筒之男大神), Sokotsutsu no O no Ōkami (底筒之男大神), Okinagaranushihime no Ōkami (息長足姫大神), Onamochi no Ōkami (大名持大神), [[Sukunabikona]] no Ōkami (少彦名大神) and others are enshrined here. ==Overview== {{Citation needed|date=March 2024}} '''Sumiyoshi Shrine''' (住吉神社, Sumiyoshi Jinja) is a Shinto shrine located in [[Otaru]], [[Hokkaido]], dedicated to the Sumiyoshi deities (Sokotutsuo no Kami, Nakatutsuo no Kami, Uwatsutsuo no Kami, and Okitsuhime no Mikoto). It is currently classified as a Beppyo Shrine under the [[Association of Shinto Shrines]], and its former Rank under the modern shrine Ranking system was Prefectural Shrine. == History == * [[1865]] (Keio 1): Shigeakira Kikuchi, a priest from Hakodate Hachiman Shrine, petitioned the Hakodate Bugyōsho to enshrine the Sumiyoshi deities as the general guardians for the Otarunai and Takashima areas. Permission was granted to enshrine them in Otarunai. * [[1868]] (Meiji 1): The sacred objects arrived in Otarunai. Due to the unprepared shrine grounds, they were temporarily enshrined at Itsukushima Shrine, where the consecration ceremony took place. * [[1875]] (Meiji 8): The shrine was classified as a Gōsha (village shrine). * [[1881]] (Meiji 14): The shrine buildings were constructed at the current location, and the shrine was relocated there. * [[1896]] (Meiji 29): The shrine was renamed from "Suminoe Shrine" to "Sumiyoshi Shrine". * [[1906]] (Meiji 39): The shrine was elevated to Prefectural Shrine status. * [[1955]] (Showa 30): The shrine was added to the list of Beppyo Shrines under the Association of Shinto Shrines. * [[1971]] (Showa 46): To commemorate the 100th anniversary of its establishment, the shrine buildings were renovated. * [[1997]] (Heisei 9): To mark the 130th anniversary, the shrine’s mikoshi (portable shrine), the largest in Hokkaido, was restored. * [[2007]] (Heisei 19): To celebrate the 140th anniversary, a mikoshi warehouse was built to house five mikoshi and one horse-drawn carriage. == annual Grand festival == The Sumiyoshi Shrine annual Grand festival, commonly known as the "Otaru festival," is considered one of the three major festivals in Otaru.{{Cite web | url = http://www.hokkaido-np.co.jp/news/chiiki/551341.html | title = 小樽・住吉神社例大祭 露店150店連なる 16日まで | work = [[Hokkaido Shimbun]] | publisher = Hokkaido Shimbunsha | date = 2014-07-15 | access-date = 2014-07-16 | archive-url = https://web.archive.org/web/20140726221521/https://www.hokkaido-np.co.jp/news/chiiki/551341.html | archive-date = 2014-07-26 }} == Access == * [[JR Hokkaido]] [[Hakodate Main Line]] [[Minami-Otaru Station]], an 8-minute walk * [[Hokkaido Chuo Bus]] (Otaru Depot, Shin Sapporo Depot, Satsuru Line), [[JR Hokkaido Bus]] (Satsuru Line), "Sumiyoshi Shrine Mae" bus stop, a 1-minute walk == Gallery == Sumiyoshi Shrine of Otaru - Shamusho.jpg|Office (Otaru City Designated Historical Building) Sumiyoshi Shrine of Otaru - Haiden.jpg|Worship Hall Kataro Matsukawa 20210919.jpg|Statue of Katataro Matsukawa Monument to National Foundation Day at Sumiyoshi Shrine of Otaru.jpg|Monument to National Foundation Day Monument to Kitchen Knives at Sumiyoshi Shrine of Otaru.jpg|Kitchen Knife Monument Height Mesurement Stone at Sumiyoshi Shrine of Otaru.jpg|Height Measurement Stone and Child Blessing Dog File:第二次世界大戦前の住吉神社（北海道小樽市）.jpg|Sumiyoshi Shrine approach in the Taisho era (postcard dated July 9, 1919) == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat}} {{Osm box|w|665046858}} * {{Official website|url=http://www.otarusumiyoshijinja.or.jp/|name=Otaru General Guardian "Sumiyoshi Shrine"}} - revision.text:
{{Short description|Shrine in Hokkaido, Japan}} '''Sumiyoshi Shrine''' (住吉神社, ''Sumiyoshi Jinja'') is a [[Shinto]] shrine located in [[:en:Otaru, Hokkaido|Otaru, Hokkaido]]. Its annual festival is on July 15.<ref>https://unga-plus.com/?pid=172674544</ref> The [[kami]] Kamitsusu no O no Ōkami (上筒之男大神), Nakatsutsu no O no Ōkami (中筒之男大神), Sokotsutsu no O no Ōkami (底筒之男大神), Okinagaranushihime no Ōkami (息長足姫大神), Onamochi no Ōkami (大名持大神), [[Sukunabikona]] no Ōkami (少彦名大神) and others are enshrined here.
==See also== *[[List of Shinto shrines in Japan#Hokkaid.C5.8D|List of Shinto shrines in Hokkaidō]]
==References== {{Reflist}}
==External links== *[https://hokkaidojinjacho.jp/住三吉神社/ Hokkaido Shinto listing]

Its annual festival is on July 15.<ref>https://unga-plus.com/?pid=172674544</ref> The [[kami]] Kamitsusu no O no Ōkami (上筒之男大神), Nakatsutsu no O no Ōkami (中筒之男大神), Sokotsutsu no O no Ōkami (底筒之男大神), Okinagaranushihime no Ōkami (息長足姫大神), Onamochi no Ōkami (大名持大神), [[Sukunabikona]] no Ōkami (少彦名大神) and others are enshrined here.
==See also== *[[List of Shinto shrines in Japan#Hokkaid.C5.8D|List of Shinto shrines in Hokkaidō]]
==References== {{Reflist}}
==External links== *[https://hokkaidojinjacho.jp/住三吉神社/ Hokkaido Shinto listing]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Sumiyoshi-jinja (Otaru, Hokkaido)]][[en:Sumiyoshi Shrine (Hokkaidō)]][[ja:住吉神社 (小樽市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Sumiyoshi-jinja (Otaru, Hokkaido)]][[en:Sumiyoshi Shrine (Hokkaidō)]][[ja:住吉神社 (小樽市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Sumiyoshi-jinja (Otaru, Hokkaido)]][[en:Sumiyoshi Shrine (Hokkaidō)]][[ja:住吉神社 (小樽市)]] {{Short description|Shrine in Hokkaido, Japan}} '''Sumiyoshi Shrine''' (住吉神社, ''Sumiyoshi Jinja'') is a [[Shinto]] shrine located in [[:en:Otaru, Hokkaido|Otaru, Hokkaido]]. Its annual festival is on July 15.<ref>https://unga-plus.com/?pid=172674544</ref> The [[kami]] Kamitsusu no O no Ōkami (上筒之男大神), Nakatsutsu no O no Ōkami (中筒之男大神), Sokotsutsu no O no Ōkami (底筒之男大神), Okinagaranushihime no Ōkami (息長足姫大神), Onamochi no Ōkami (大名持大神), [[Sukunabikona]] no Ōkami (少彦名大神) and others are enshrined here.
==See also== *[[List of Shinto shrines in Japan#Hokkaid.C5.8D|List of Shinto shrines in Hokkaidō]]
